descriptions:
['The #Whole is a single entity or an aggregate of entities which separates into #Parts.

<ex><fex name="Whole">Metalworkers  union</fex> to <t>split</t> into regional divisions</ex>', 'This refers collectively to the Parts resulting from separation of a Whole:

<ex>These daughter chromosomes then begin to <t>separate</t> <fex name="Parts">from each other</fex>, each moving away from the metaphase plate and toward one of the two spindle pole regions.</ex>
<ex>Watch as they <t>split</t> <fex name="Parts">into many differently-shaped bits</fex>.</ex>', 'Part-1 is the first of two Parts mentioned.

<ex>Initially, <fex name="Part_1">the front part</fex> <t>separated</t> from the rest of the body.</ex>', 'Part_2 is the second of two Parts mentioned. This is usually a PP Complement. 

<ex>Initially the front <t>split</t> <fex name="Part_2">from the rest of the body</fex></ex>', 'This frame element expresses a property of the Parts or components of the Whole which correlates with the separation. Criterion is typically expressed as a PP headed by <ment>by</ment>, or as a complex PP of the form <ment>on the basis of</ment>.... 
<ex>Particles will therefore only <t>separate</t> <fex name="Crit">on the basis of contact angle</fex> as long as their mass is between an upper and lower critical value.</ex>', 'Manner of performing an action', 'Degree to which event occurs', 'An intermediate action or event that enables the separation.

<ex>Explore how cells <t>divide</t> <fex name="Mns">by undergoing mitosis</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Time when the separation occurs.

<ex>However it is believed they <t>separated</t> <fex name="Time">at least 2 if not 3 million years ago</fex>.</ex>.', 'Where the separation occurs.

<ex>The two parts likely <t>separated</t> <fex name="Place">above the Atlantic</fex>.</ex>']

descriptions:
['The #Artist works off-stage to help make the #Production.
<ex><fex name="Artist">John Williams</fex> <t>composed</t> the soundtrack to Star Wars.</ex>', 'The #Production is the final product put out by the off-stage (and on-stage) participants.
<ex>Ralph Smith <t>produced</t> <fex name="Prod">the off-Broadway hit "Why Is My Name Ralph?"</fex>.</ex>', 'An #Artist may be categorizedy by #Type.
<ex><fex name="Type">Hollywood</fex> <t>director</t></ex>
<ex><fex name="Type">independent</fex> <t>film-maker</t></ex>', 'Often (especially with film or television) the #Production is advertised and transmitted to the audience by the #Distributor.
<ex>Even desperate producers would never <t>produce</t> a film <fex name="Dist">for that television station</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE indicates the particular orgainzaion that makes the #Production.
<ex>The latest performance <t>produced</t> <fex name="Studio">by The Umbrella Thesbians</fex> was a major hit in France.</ex>
<ex>First layer: <fex name="Studio">New Line Cinema</fex> <t>produces</t> several films each year.</ex>
<ex>Second layer: <fex name="Artist">New Line Cinema</fex> <t>produces</t> several films each year.</ex>', 'This FE indicates the #Medium in which a particular #Artist works or a #Production is made.
<ex>He <t>produces</t> <fex name="Medium">for television</fex></ex>
<ex>She is a <fex name="Medium">theatre</fex> <t>director</t>.</ex>', 'In screen performances, the #Performer is filmed as he or she acts out a role with respect to the larger #Production
<ex>This particular scene was <t>shot</t> <fex name="Perf">with a local choir and dancers</fex>.</ex>', 'The music which may be performed in the performance of the #Production, either in isolation or accompanying actions and speech.', 'A #Scene is a sub-part of a larger production.  In film, scenes are sometimes recorded independently, and later edited into the final movie.', 'This FE indicates the time when the #Production is made.
<ex>Silence of the Lambs was <t>filmed</t> <fex name="Time">in 1991</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE indicates the location of production.
<ex>The documentary was <t>shot</t> <fex name="Place">on location</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE indicates the #Nationality, ethnicity, or primary working location of the #Artist.
<ex>Several <fex name="Nationality">Canadian</fex> <t>directors</t> have received attention from critics in recent years.</ex>
<ex><fex name="Nationality">Local</fex> <t>film-makers</t> often collaborate for larger efforts.</ex>', 'The #Purpose for which an #Artist works on a #Production.
<ex>I <t>produced</t> this film <fex name="Purpose">to let people know what I went through as a child</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Manner in which the #Artist creates the #Production.']

descriptions:
['The #Agent is a sentient being who physically controls the movement of the #Theme via the carrier, accompanying the #Theme.
<ex><fex name="Agent">Karl</fex> <t>carried</t> the books across the campus to the library.</ex>', '#Goal identifies the endpoint of the path.  

<ex>Karl <t>carried</t> the books <fex name="goa">to the library</fex>.</ex>', '#Source indicates the beginning of the path along which the #Theme travels.  

<ex>Karl <t>hauled</t> the books <fex name="sou">from the library</fex>  to the office.</ex>', 'Path along which carrying occurs.
<ex>Karl <t>carried</t> the books <fex name="Path">across the campus</fex>.</ex>', 'The objects being carried.
<ex>Karl <t>toted</t> <fex name="Theme">the books</fex> to the car.</ex>', '#Area is used for descriprion of a general area in which the carrying action takes place when the motion is understood to be irregular or not to consist of a single, linear path.', 'The #Carrier provides support for the #Theme.  Movement of the #Carrier results in movement of the #Theme.
<ex><fex name="Car">The boat</fex> <t>ferried</t> the troops across the river.</ex>', 'The #Constant_location expresses the location of the #Theme relative to the #Carrier.  This relationship holds for the entire length of the carrying event.

<ex>Karl <t>took</t> the books to the library <fex name="CL">in his car</fex>.</ex>
<ex>Karl <t>carried</t> the books to the library <fex name="CL">in his arms</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Distance the #Theme is carried.', 'This FE identifies the #Manner  in which the #Carrier or #Agent carries the #Theme .', 'The #Speed is the rate at which the carrying or transporting occurs.', 'The #Purpose for which the #Carrier or #Agent carries the #Theme . <ex>We had to <t>carry</t> the heavy weights all the way to the top of the hill<fex name="Pps">to show our strength</fex>.</ex>', 'The actions the #Agent takes to move the #Theme.
<ex>The books were <t>taken</t> to the library <fex name="Mns">by helicopter deliveries</fex>.</ex>
<ex>I <t>haul</t> books <fex name="Mns">by putting them on top of my head</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Place is the general area that serves as a setting for the specific motion of the #Agent and #Theme (with #Source, #Path, or #Goal).  
<ex><fex name="Place">Even in such a small port</fex>, these boats would <t>transport</t> many tons of trade-goods in to shore annually.</ex>', 'The time in which the carrying event takes place.']

descriptions:
['This FE identifies the #Number.', 'It identifies the #Entity enumerated by the #Number.', "A #Unit expresses some quantification of some property of an entity, event, or situation by providing a base value of the property combined with a #Number expressing how the current value for the property relates to the #Unit.

She's not even SIX years old yet!

As units that specify a quantity property, numbers themselves can be used as Units.

THREE dozen eggs makes eighteen standard omelettes .", 'A #Unit expresses some quantification of some property of an entity, event, or situation by providing a base value of the property combined with a #Number expressing how the current value for the property relates to the #Unit.  

<ex>She's not even <t>six</t> <fex name="Unit">years</fex> old yet!</ex>

As units that specify a quantity property, numbers themselves can be used as #Units.

<ex><t>Three</t> <fex name="Unit">dozen</fex> eggs makes eighteen standard omelettes .</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Precision to which an #Entity approximates a #Number.

About THIRTY cases are known.

In the case where a number is modified by a #Multiplier, we have found it more plausible that phrases expressing #Precision modify the main number (thus "million" in the first example, not "two"):

JP has almost two MILLION dollars.

There are just over six BILLION people in the world today.', 'How many times larger the group of #Entities is than the base #Number.', "The total number of instances of the #Entity. This FE should be used when more basic number expressions are used together to form a #Composite_number whose value is the sum of the constituent numbers. It is annotated on a second FE layer. For cases where a number is used to multiply another number, use #Multiplier.

She found six HUNDRED and twelve errors in the manuscript.

Four score and SEVEN years ago , I wasn't even born.

THIRTY seven senators refused to vote."]

descriptions: ['The #Agent blocks access to the #Useful_location.', 'The #Useful_location is the place the Theme is headed towards and the #Agent has changed the accessibility of.', 'The #Agent may regulate access to the #Useful_location by regulating access to a #Portal, including a part of the portal that could block potential #Themes, such as doors or gates.  ', 'The #Theme is headed towards, or has an intention or tendency to heard towards, the #Useful_location, but is potentially blocked due to actions of the #Agent.', 'The degree to which the #Useful_location is or is not accessible.', 'Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the #Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).', 'An action carried out by the #Agent to bring about the change in accessibility.', 'The time when the #Agent changes the accessibility of the #Useful_location.', 'The general place where the blocking or allowing of access occurs.', 'The purpose for which the Agent changes the accessibility of the #Useful_location', 'The #Agent uses the #Instrument to block access, often by placing the #Instrument such that it cannot be traversed.']

descriptions:
['This is the object which moves. In many sentences this FE will be expressed as something which moves under its own power. However, this FE is not <ment>required</ment> to be an animate mover, so it is different from a self-mover.

<ex>At that point <fex name="Thm">the truck in front of me</fex> <t>turned</t> right into the huge ditch/field that separates the interstate from the access road.</ex>', 'The new direction in which the #Theme continues its motion.

<ex>Eventually, though, I backtracked to Kel Tole and <m>hung</m> a <t><fex name="dir">left</fex></t> toward Kilgal Tole.</ex>', 'Any expression which implies a definite starting-point of motion expresses the frame element #Source. 

<ex>One of those ugly landcuisers <t>cut</t> left <fex name="Src">from the opposite lane</fex> in moving traffic.</ex>', 'Any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a #Source nor a #Goal (see below) expresses the frame element #Path, excluding directional expressions.

<ex>They <t>turned</t> right <fex name="Path">through another gate</fex> into a courtyard opening.</ex>', 'Any expression which indicates where the #Theme ends up as a result of the change of direction. 

<ex>Finally, where the road begins to climb steeply, <t>cut</t> right <fex name="Goal">onto a small trail near a sign in a tree</fex>, and go downhill to the stream.</ex>.', 'Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including force (<ment>hard</ment>, <ment>softly</ment>), secondary effects (<ment>quietly</ment>, <ment>loudly</ment>), and general descriptions comparing events (<ment>the same way</ment>). In agentive frames, it may indicate salient characteristics of an intentional Theme that affect the action (<ment>presumptuously</ment>, <ment>coldly</ment>, <ment>deliberately</ment>, <ment>eagerly</ment>, <ment>carefully</ment>).

<ex>I <fex name="Manner">carefully</fex> <t>turned</t> left onto Narragansett and worked my way through the debris.</ex>', 'An event involving the #Theme that results in the #Theme moving in the specified way.', 'The #Speed is the rate at which the #Theme changes direction.

<ex>Suddenly, the jeep <fex name="Speed">quickly</fex> <t>turned</t> left down a side road.</ex>', 'The #Place is the setting where the change of direction during the Theme s motion occurs.

<ex><t>Turn</t> right <fex name="Place">at the stop light</fex> and follow Park Avenue.</ex>', 'The configuration formed by the #Path that the #Theme traverses during the change of direction.

<ex>The tank rumbled, backed quickly, <t>turned</t> left and right <fex name="Path_shape">in a sine curve</fex>.</ex>', 'The time at which the change of direction takes place.


<ex><fex name="Time">Then</fex> I <t>turned</t> left onto 220th Street.</ex>', 'The angle between the old and the new #Direction in which the #Theme moves.

<ex>There we <t>turned</t> right <fex name="ang">by almost 180 degrees</fex> and by a winding road, going steeply
up we went to And?lka.</ex>']

descriptions:
['This is the object which moves. It may be an entity which moves under its own power, but it need not be. <ex><fex name="Theme">The officer</fex> <t>left</t> the house.</ex>', 'All the verbs in this frame express some change of location, away from one place and to another. Any constituent that expresses the initial position of the #Theme, before the change of location, is tagged with #Source. Often the #Source is understood from context. <ex>The woman <t>left</t>. <fex name="Source">DNI</fex></ex> <ex>The woman <t>left</t> <fex name="Source">the house</fex>.</ex><ex>We <t>departed</t> <fex name="Source">from New York</fex> on Friday.</ex>', 'Any description of the trajectory of the departure which is neither a #Source nor a #Goal expresses the frame element #Path.  In this frame, #Path expressions almost always have a sense meaning  via . 
<ex>Spiderman <t>left</t> <fex name="Path">through the window</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE labels any expression which tells where the #Theme ends up, or would end up, as a result of departing from the #Source. <ex>Our visitors <t>left</t> <fex name="Goal">for Los Angeles</fex>.</ex>', 'Any expression which describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion expresses the frame element #Manner. Descriptions of steadiness, grace, and many other things count as #Manner expressions, though #Speed and #Means should be tagged using the more specific FE labels. <ex>The messenger <t>left</t> the room <fex name="Manner">clumsily</fex>.</ex>', 'The action of the #Theme which results in its departure. <ex>He <t>retreated</t> from his opponent <fex name="Means">by taking a quick leap backwards</fex>.</ex>', 'This frame element labels words or phrases that describe how quickly the #Theme is moving. <ex>He <fex name="Speed">quickly</fex> <t>exited</t> the crowded terminal.</ex>', 'The #Mode_of_transportation expresses how the motion of the #Theme is effected, by its body or by a vehicle which holds and conveys the #Theme. Vehicles can move in any way and in any medium. 

In this frame, #Mode_of_transportation is often expressed obliquely in a prepositional phrase headed by  in  or  by .

<ex>We <t>set</t> <t>out</t> <fex name="Mode_of_transportation">by boat</fex> for Nova Scotia.
</ex><ex>Wilbur <t>departed</t> <fex name="Mode_of_transportation">on foot</fex> for London.</ex>', 'The distance that the #Theme has traveled. <ex>He <t>withdrew</t> <fex name="Distance">a little</fex> from her. </ex>', 'The #Time at which the departure takes place.<ex>We <t>left</t> <fex name="Time">in the morning</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Place is the location in which the departing takes place.', 'This FE identifies the #Purpose for which the #Theme departs from the #Source.']

descriptions: ['In cases where the #Interlocutors involved in a conversation are construed as two separate groups with different levels of volitionality or control, this FE identifies the conversational participant with greater semantic and syntactic prominence, as compared with #Interlocutor_2. That is, #Interlocutor_1 is generally encoded as Subject, and peripheral FEs that do not make reference to the event itself generally make reference to #Interlocutor_1.  Thus a #Purpose (or #Means, #Depictive, or #Manner) is construed to be that of #Interlocutor_1, not necessarily that of #Interlocutor_2 or the group of #Interlocutors as a whole.', 'In cases where the #Interlocutors involved in a conversation are construed as two separate groups with different levels of volitionality or control, #Interlocutor_2 refers to the semantically and grammatically less prominent group.', 'In some sentences both participants are expressed by the subject. In some sentences, the subject is assigned the #Interlocutors. In such sentences, the subject always denotes more than one person, and it is understood that the reciprocal communication takes place between these two (or more) people. The subject may denote two or more people either through conjunction or through plurality, or by denoting a group. In each of these cases the #Interlocutors is assigned.', 'It expresses the subject that the #Interlocutors are trying to come to some agreement about. descriptions:
['The #Rule is the set of constrants on behavior that the #Agent implements (i.e., forces others to obey or face consequences).

<ex>Employers should strictly <t>enforce</t> <fex name="Rule">a prohibition against use of any substance that may affect a worker s physical or mental ability</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Agent, often an institution, governmental organization, or arm thereof, puts into effect (i.e., enforces) a #Rule.

<ex>Can <fex name="Agent">a landlord</fex> <target>enforce</target> rules upon tenants?</ex>', 'This FE describes the medium through which the rule was enforced.  It may occur in the same syntactic position as the Agent.
<ex>The rules will be <t>enforced</t> <fex name="Medium">by a new computer system that tracks people s movements</fex>.</ex>', 'The length of time for which the #Rule is or is not in effect.

<ex>The coat-and-tie dress code for the Plimsoll Club in the World Trade Center was strictly <t>enforced</t> <fex name="dur">for years</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Time at which the Agent enforces the #Rule.

<ex>That particular regulation was only <t>enforced</t> <fex name="tim">in 1920</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Place at which the #Rule is or is not enforced.

<ex>For this reason they <t>enforced</t> the settlement system <fex name="place">in America</fex> just as strictly as in Germany.</ex>', 'The #Manner describes some state of the #Agent, or provides additional characterization of the event.

<ex>The coat-and-tie dress code for the Plimsoll Club in the World Trade Center was <fex name="Manner">strictly</fex> <t>enforced</t> for years.</ex>', 'The #Means is an intermediate action done by the #Agent in order to enforce the #Rule.

<ex>Since 1961 the church has <t>enforced</t> it <fex name="mea">by "disfellowshipping" or expelling un-repentant members who wilfully accept prohibited blood components</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Purpose for which the #Agent enforces the #Rule.']

descriptions:
['The #Escapee manages to leave the #Undesirable_location.
<ex><fex name="Escapee">Benjamin</fex> <t>escaped</t> from Germany.</ex>', 'The <fex name="">Undesirable_location</fex> is where the Escapee begins.

<ex>Benjamin <t>escaped</t> <fex name="Undesirable_location"> from Germany</fex>.</ex>', '#Goal identifies any expression which tells where the Escapee ends up, or would end up, as a result of the motion. 
<ex>Benjamin <t>escaped</t> <fex name="Goal">to the United States</fex>.</ex>', '<fex name="Means"></fex> covers any act performed by the #Escapee brings about the escape. 

<ex>Benjamin <t>escaped</t> from Germany <fex name="Means">by bribing the border guards</fex>.</ex>', '#Manner is used for any expression which describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion, including descriptions of steadiness, grace, means of motion, etc.

<ex>Benjamin <t>escaped</t> from Germany <fex name="Manner">with difficulty</fex>.</ex>', '#Speed idenitifies the rate at which the escaping occurs.

<ex>Benjmain <fex name="Speed">speedily</fex> <t>escaped</t>  from Germany.</ex>', '#Vehicle identifies the means of conveyance by which the #Escapee leaves the #Undesirable_location.

<ex>Benjamin <t>escaped</t> <fex name="Vehicle">in a lorrie</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Time when the escaping occurs.', 'This FE identifies the #Purpose for which the escaping takes place.
<ex>Benjamin <t>escaped</t> from Germany <fex name="Purpose">to rsave his life</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Place where the Escaping occurs.', '#Path describes the trajectory of motion of the #Escapee.

<ex>Benjmain <t>escaped</t> from Germany <fex name="Path">along the southern border</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the (abstract) nearness of the #Escapee to being caught in the #Undesirable_location.

<ex>Benjamin s <fex name="Degr">narrow</fex> <t>escape</t> from Germany left him fearful.</ex>', 'The distance along a path away from the #Undesirable_location that the #Escapee travels.

<ex>She <t>escaped</t> <fex name="dis">a few feet</fex> away, then looked back at him coldly.</ex>']

descriptions: ['In cases where the #Exchangers involved in a conversation areconstrued as two separate groups with different levels ofvolitionality or control, this FE identifies the conversationalparticipant with greater semantic and syntactic prominence, ascompared with #Exchanger_2. That is, #Exchanger_1 is generallyencoded as Subject, and peripheral FEs that do not make referenceto the event itself generally make reference to #Exchanger_1. Thus a #Purpose (or #Means, #Depictive, or #Manner) is construed to be that of #Exchanger_1, not necessarily that of #Exchanger_2 or the groupof #Exchangers as a whole.', 'In cases where the #Exchangers involved in a conversation areconstrued as two separate groups with different levels ofvolitionality or control, #Exchanger_2 refers to the semanticallyand grammatically less prominent group. It is generally encodedwithin a prepositional phrase. Note that itis possible for a single role to correspond to multiple referents.', 'In some sentences both participants are expressed by the Subject. In sentences like this, the Subject is assigned the FE role #Exchangers. In such sentences, the Subject always denotes more than one person, and it is understood that the reciprocal communication takes place between these two (or more) people. The Subject may denote two or more people either through conjunction, or through plurality or by denoting a group. In each of these cases the FE #Exchangers is assigned.', 'The item that begins in the possession of #Exchanger_1.', 'The item that begins in the possession of #Exchanger_2.', 'The items which change possession between the #Exchangers.', 'It identifies the place where the exchange occurs.', 'It identifies the purpose for which #Exchanger_1 or the #Exchangers make the trade.', 'It identifies the time when the exchange takes place.', 'It identifies the Means by which #Exchanger_1 or the #Exchangers affect the trade.', 'Any description of the exchange act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects, and general descriptions comparing events. descriptions:
['The person who inspects the #Ground to see if it is intact or without blemish.', 'The entity that the #Inspector inspects with his senses to ascertain whether it is intact.', 'An entity whose presence would impair the acceptability or usefulness of the #Ground.

<ex>Security guards <t>frisked</t> all the spectators <fex name="unw">for weapons</fex> as they entered the stadium.</ex>', 'Some of the words in this frame occur with a constituent that expresses the desired outcome of the inspection.  Normally this constituent is a PP Complement headed by <ment>for</ment>, or a <ment>to</ment>-marked VP Complement.

<ex>Leather is <fex name="man">carefully</fex> <t>inspected</t> for softness, elasticity and texture</ex>', 'This FE identifies the extent  to which the #Inspector examines the #Ground.

<ex> This section was <fex name="deg">partially</fex> <t>inspected</t> by the commissioner of Metro rail safety on Thursday</ex>', 'The #Time when the #Inspector examines the #Ground.

descriptions:
['The #Agent is the person (or other force) that installs the #Component.


<ex><fex name="Agt">John</fex> <t>installed</t> it last night on my Gentoo system and everything is going fine so far.</ex>', 'The #Component is the object that is placed in a #Fixed_location.


<ex>My plumber <t>installed</t> <fex name="com">the shower pan</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Fixed_location is the location where the #Component is placed and to which it is attached so as to be useful.


<ex>I assembled the fuel tank using a vent line and a supply line, and then I <t>installed</t>
the tank <fex name="fix">in the fuselage</fex>.</ex>', 'Any description of the installation event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Agent that affect the action (<ment>deliberately</ment>, <ment>eagerly</ment>, <ment>carefully</ment>).', 'The #Means is an act whereby the #Agent achieves the installation of the #Component.


<ex>We <t>installed</t> the spindle assembly <fex name="Means">by starting with the lower-ball joint, followed by the upper</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Place indicates where the installation occurs.

<ex>When I <t>installed</t> the server <fex name="Place">there</fex> last winter they only had two rows of racks, mostly empty.</ex>', 'The time period in which the #Component is installed.


<ex>When we <t>installed</t> our hood <fex name="Time">last month</fex>, the folks at Dallas Auto Body painted our roll pan at the same time.</ex>', 'The #Area is the setting into which multiple #Components or a  complex #Component is installed.    <ex>I <t>installed</t>  tongue and groove pine flooring <fex name="Area">through my entire house</fex>.</ex>']

['The person who is committed to a facility with a view towards helping them mentally or physically.



<ex>Buffy s got this hospital phobia because her caring parents <t>institutionalized</t>
<fex name="pat">her</fex> for a while before she moved to Sunnydale.</ex>', 'The facility where the #Patient is taken for treatment.


<ex>Unfortunately for Mrs. Lincoln, her son, Robert, <t>institutionalized</t> her <fex name="fac">in the
Batavia Asylum</fex> shortly after the visit to St. Charles.</ex>', 'The #Authority is the actor carrying out the institutionalization with proper legal authorization, either invested into them in general by the government or granted due to power of attorney over the #Patient.

<ex><fex name="Time">Later</fex> her mother became depressed and was <t>institutionalized</t>.</ex>', 'An intentional action performed by the #Authority that brings about the institutionalization of the #Patient.

<ex>Without informing Volkobraun or her attorney of the court s decision, police

It may also indicate salient characteristics of the #Authority that affect the action (<ment>ruthlessly</ment>). 


<ex>St Remy, of course, is most noted for being the place where Van Gogh <t>committed</t>
himself <fex name="man">voluntarily</fex> to an asylum.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the length of the time period for which the #Authority has placed  the #Patient in the #Facility.

<ex>This time, physicians <t>hospitalized</t> him <fex name="dur">for more than a month</fex>.</ex>', 'The location in which the #Facility is situated.

<ex>The widow of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. suffered a stroke on Wednesday, and was
<t>hospitalized</t> <fex name="pla">in Atlanta</fex>.</ex>']

descriptions:
['The #Speaker is the person who conventionally uses a linguistic sign to refer to an #Entity.

<ex><fex name="spe">They</fex> <t>call</t> it a Mutt Mitt.</ex>', 'The concrete or abstract thing with which the #Speaker has associated a particular linguistic sign.

to 11,000-year-old specimens</ex>', 'The linguistic sign that the #Speaker uses to designate the #Entity. 

<ex>In Texas, we <t>call</t> it <fex name="lab">walking"</fex>.</ex>

<ex><fex name="tim">Back in the olden days</fex>, they <t>called</t> it "Bohemian people."</ex>', 'A physical space whose occupants use the #Label to refer to the #Entity.

descriptions:
['This FE identifies the rule designed to guide activity, deprecating some types of activity and/or requiring other types of activity. This FE is incorporated into each LU in this frame.', 'The state-of-affairs which is disallowed by the #Law.

<ex>I think we need a <t>law</t> <fex name="for">against all firearms within city limits</fex>.</ex>', 'A state-of-affairs which the #Law declares must be maintained.

<ex>The <t>law</t> <fex name="req">that all minors must be accompanied by an adult</fex> is a laughingstock.</ex>', 'The use for which a law is intended.

<ex>We brought back a <t>law</t> <fex name="Use">to forbid the use of profanity</fex>.</ex>', 'An individual or individuals that bring the #Law into existence.

<ex>But this was forbidden by the <fex name="Creator">King s</fex> <t>Law</t> of 1665.</ex>', 'The time at which a #Law comes into existence.

<ex>The <fex name="Time">1996</fex> welfare reform <t>act</t> was a sham.</ex>', 'The term used to refer to a #Law.

<ex>Well, they re still in violation of the <fex name="Name">Sarbanes-Oxley</fex> <t>Act</t>.</ex>', 'An indication of the subtype of #Law.

<ex>According to <fex name="Type">Canon</fex> <t>Law</t>, priests may not marry.</ex>', 'Any indication of what makes up the #Law.

<ex>We don t need another <t>law</t> <fex name="Part">with 300 subsections</fex>.</ex>', 'The location where a given #Law is in force.

<ex>According to <fex name="jur">Fijian</fex> <t>law</t>, no-one may enter the park after sunset.</ex>']

This FE is for expressions that indicate more specific purposes than the general one(s) indicated by the target itself.', 'This FE describes the general location where the #Agency operates, but does not indicate the jurisdiction of the #Agency.']

descriptions:
['The #Leader is the one who has the role of leading.  With verbs, and with predicate nominals linking an individual to a #Role, the #Leader is usually the External Argument: 
<ex><fex name="Leader">King Hussein</fex> <t>ruled</t> Jordan.</ex> 
<ex><fex name="Leader">Hussein</fex> was <t>King</t> of Jordan.</ex> 
With leadership nouns, the frame element #Leader is most frequently instantiated by the target itself.
<ex>The <fex name="Leader"><t>queen</t></fex> announced her upcoming visit to Belgium.</ex>', 'The role played by the #Leader in a given situation, often an official title; incorporated in most noun LUsin this frame. When expressed separately from the target, #Role usually occurs in a PP Complement headed by <ment>as</ment>.

<ex>Hussein <t>reigned</t> <fex name="Role">as King of Jordan</fex></ex>', 'The entity whose actions or beliefs the #Leader directs in the context of a joint activity or movement.  This entity can be a political body, a person, or a group of people.

<ex>Cyprus Turks turn against <fex name="gov">their</fex> <t>leader</t></ex>
<ex>Naoto Kan is the new <t>leader</t> <fex name="gov">of the Democratic Party of Japan</fex>.</ex>
<ex>King Hussein <t>ruled</t> <fex name="gov">Jordan</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Leader directs the #Activity.

<ex>The captain s cousin <t>led</t> <fex name="Activity">the mutiny</fex>.</ex>
<ex>John Cage is one of the <t>leaders</t> <fex name="Activity">of the minimalist music movement</fex>.</ex>', 'Leadership by any particular individual is frequently limited in time, which is noted here as #Duration.   #Duration usually occurs in a PP Complement.
<ex>She <t>reigned</t> <fex name="Duration">for 3 years</fex>.</ex>', '#Manner refers to the style/way in which the leadership is asserted.

<ex>Alternative and orthodox practitioners <t>reign</t> <fex name="Manner">harmoniously</fex> <fex name="Manner">side by side</fex>.</ex>', '#Means identifies act(s) performed by the #Leader through which leadership is maintained.
<ex>He <t>ruled</t> <fex name="Mns">by sheer brutality and intimidation</fex>.</ex>
<ex>Napolean <t>reigned</t> <fex name="Mns">by the grace of God and the will of the people</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Degree to which leadership is asserted.', '#Time refers to the point in time that the leadership took place.

<ex>The general <t>led</t> his troops into battle <fex name="Time">on June 22, 1883</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Descriptor is a temporary condition of the #Leader.

<ex>A <fex name="des">nervous</fex> <t>governor</t> stepped up to the lectern to give his speech.</ex>', 'This FE indicates what subtype of the general category the #Leader belongs to.', 'The locale from which the #Leader leads the #Governed or the #Activity.

<ex>He <t>ruled</t> the west <fex name="Place">from his palace in Milan</fex>.</ex>']

descriptions:
['The Authority (which may be the jury, judge, magistrate or court) decides on the #Finding.

<ex><fex name="Authority">The US International Trade Commission</fex> <t>ruled</t> <fex name="Finding">that a 1987 anti-dumping tariff against frozen concentrated orange juice from Brazil could be revoked</fex> <fex name="Case">DNI</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the outcome of the #Authority s deliberation or consideration.

<ex>Miss Mahmod s father <t>ruled</t> <fex name="Finding">that she would never marry him</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE describes the question on which the #Authority gives a #Finding.

<ex>US Supreme Court <t>ruled</t> <fex name="Case">on flag salutes in public schools</fex> today.</ex>', 'The person about whom the #Authority passes a judgment or #Finding.

<ex>They held <t>judgment</t> <fex name="D">upon me</fex> as a convicted traitress.</ex>', 'When the Authority gives his #Finding.', 'Where the #Finding takes place.', 'This FE indicates the basis in law of a #Finding.', 'This FE identifies the #Manner in which the #Authority acts.', 'This FE identifies the purpose why the #Authority returns a particular #Finding.', 'This FE identifies an action that the #Authority performs in order to return the verdict.', 'This FE presents the current frame as an integral part of a larger conceptualization expressed by another frame. The frame expressed by the target typically corresponds to a Means action within the frame expressed by the Re-encoding, but not necessarily']

descriptions:
['The person or institution who receives the #Theme from the #Lender for a #Duration.
<ex>I <t>lent</t> the book <fex name="Borr">to him</fex> for a whole month, and he still hasn t read it.</ex>', 'The person or institution who gives the #Theme to the #Borrower for a #Duration.

<ex><fex name="Lend">I</fex> <t>lent</t> the book to him for a whole month, and he still hasn t read it.</ex>', 'The object that is transferred from the #Lender to the #Borrower for a #Duration.
<ex>I <t>lent</t> <fex name="Th">the book</fex> to him for a whole month, and he still hasn t read it.</ex>', 'The amout of time in which the #Borrower has possession of the #Theme.

<ex>I <t>lent</t> the book to him <fex name="Dur">for a whole month</fex>, and he still hasn t read it.</ex>', 'The aim of the #Lender which they believe will be accomplished by lending the #Theme to the #Borrower.

<ex>His parents <t>lent</t> him money <fex name="Purp">to pay off his student loans</fex>.</ex>', 'The time when the lending event occurs.

<ex>I <t>lent</t> him my car <fex name="Time">on Friday</fex>.</ex>', 'The way in which the #Lender lends the #Theme.

<ex>Juan <t>lent</t> me the car <fex name="Man">reluctantly</fex>.</ex>', 'The location in which the Lender lends the #Theme to the #Borrower.

<ex>I <t>lent</t> him my book <fex name="Pla">in Dubai</fex>.</ex>']

descriptions:
['the #exerter that can or does exert a force of the level specified by the target. 

<ex>salesmen touted the <t>power</t> <fex name="exer">of the engine's motor</fex>.</ex>', 'the #force that can or does exert a force of the level specified by the target. 

<ex>no one should underestimate the <t>mighty</t> <fex name="for">strength</fex> of the dung beetle.</ex>', 'Action', 'the #degree to which the #exerter, #action, or #force can or does exert a force. 

<ex>after being in space for six months, his muscles were <fex name="deg">extremely</fex> <t>weak</t>.</ex>

where #degree is expressed by a comparative or superlative form of the target, the fe is marked on the target (as well as on the rest of the comparative/superlative expression, if present):
<ex>years of lifting had made him a <t><fex name="deg">stronger</fex></t> man.</ex>
<ex>years of lifting had made him <t><fex name="deg">stronger</fex></t> <fex name="deg">than any of his peers</fex>.</ex>', 'the location where the #exerter, #action, or #force can or does exert its force. 
<ex>muhammad ali's <t>force</t> <fex name="pla">in the ring</fex> was indisputable.</ex>', 'this fe describes the force exertion of the #exerter, #action, or #force.

<ex>henry's <fex name="desc">longstanding</fex> <t>powerlessness</t> against the school bully made him fearful and withdrawn.</ex>', 'the #domain is the field in which the #exerter, #action, or #force can or does exert its force. 

<ex>the u.s. possesses great <fex name="dom">military</fex> <t>strength</t>.</ex>']

descriptions:
['the #resisting_entity is the item that resists the #opposing_force to the level specified by the target. 

<ex>these are made from a <t>tough</t> <fex name="ent">plastic foam</fex> and are very light to wear.</ex>

<ex>a great number of <t>hardy</t> <fex name="ent">plants</fex> usually succeed in blooming even in the severest season.</ex> 

<ex>the <t>fragile</t> <fex name="ent">canoe</fex> did not capsize!</ex>', 'the #opposing_force is the item placed in opposition to the #resisting_entity. #opposing_force hinders, blocks, helps, or allows (lets) the #resisting_entity to resist at the level specified by the target. sometimes this core fe is incorporated in the target; otherwise, it is often null instantiated in example sentences. 

<ex>the fortress was <t>impregnable</t> <fex name="opp">to attack by hostile invaders</fex>.</ex> 

<ex>its best-known feature is its soft and <t>pliable</t> duck-like beak. <fex name="opp">ini</fex> </ex>', 'this fe describes the force resistance of the #resisting_entity. 

<ex>scottish ancestors had given her an <fex name="desc">amazing</fex> <t>toughness</t> which enabled her to endure the hardships of life.</ex>', 'the #domain is the field in which the #resisting_entity resists an external force. 

<ex>factors that determine the choice of materials include <fex name="dom">physical</fex> <t>robustness</t>.</ex>']

descriptions:
['The #Agent consciously places limits upon the Range_of_options.

<ex><fex name="age">The military</fex> <t>limited</t> building access to key personnel.</ex>', 'The #Cause is a natural occurrence or state-of-affairs that limits the Range_of_options.

<ex><fex name="cau">Ongoing rains</fex> have <t>limited</t> drivers to a small number of roads.</ex>', 'The #Range_of_options would normally be expected to have a set of possibilites, but is limited by an #Agent or #Cause to particular subtypes, situations or characteristics.

<ex>The military <t>limited</t> <fex name="ran">building access</fex> to key personnel.</ex>', 'The #Characteristic indicates the subtypes or situations to which the #Range_of_options is limited.

<ex>The military <t>limited</t> access <fex name="cha">to key personnel only</fex>.</ex>

<ex>You should <t>limit</t> your meals <fex name="cha">to three per day at most</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Degree to which the #Range_of_options has been limited.

<ex>Our choices have now been <fex name="deg">severely</fex> <t>limited</t>.</ex>', 'The time at which the limiting takes place.

<ex>Banks are <fex name="tim">currently</fex> <t>limiting</t> customers to two cash transactions per day.</ex>', 'The length of time over which the #Range_of_options is limited.

<ex>If you <t>limit</t> your caloric intake <fex name="dur">for too long</fex>, it could damage your health.</ex>', 'The way in which the #Agent of #Cause limits the #Range_of_options.', 'The location in which the #Agent or #Cause limits the #Range_of_options.', 'An act performed by the Agent or Cause that accomplishes the action indicated by the target.', 'The purpose for which the Agent or Cause limits the Range_of_options.']

<ex>The shop <t>closed</t> <fex name="dur">for three hours</fex> while police did their investigation.</ex>']

['The percept which is perceived at a certain #Ground location.

<ex>The <fex name="Light"><t>glint</t></fex> of gold made me blind with greed.</ex>

Occasionally this FE shows up as a full argument:<ex>The chamber <fex name="Lit"><t>glowed</t></fex> <fex name="Lit">with brilliant light</fex>.</ex>', '#Degree to which the #Light is emitted, usually indicating the brightness.', '#Manner of #Light emission.

['The persons (or in rare cases animals) that make each other s acquaintance.

<ex><fex name="inds">My wife and I</fex> <t>met</t> in college working in the theatre and we will have been married for 30 years in June 2000.</ex>', 'The semantically more prominent one of the two parties when the Individuals are realized separately.

<ex> Gossip reports say she either <t>met</t> <fex name="ind2">Kennedy</fex> while helping him shop for suits at Calvin Klein, or was contacted by him after he saw her jogging in a New York park </ex>', 'The time when the Individuals make each other s acquaintance.

descriptions:
['The individual or individuals that form the more prominent (i.e.
agentive) party of the agreement, as compared with #Party_2. That is,
#Party_1 is generally encoded as the External Argument of verbs, and
peripheral FEs that do not make reference to the event itself
generally make reference to #Party_1. Thus #Manner is construed to be that of #Party_1, not necessarily that of #Party_2 or the group of #Parties as a whole.



<ex>On Monday, <fex name="par1">the airline</fex> reached a <t>deal</t> with the Canadian Auto Workers.</ex>', 'The semantically (and grammatically) less prominent participant (or
group of participants) in the agreement. It is generally encoded
within a prepositional phrase (e.g. headed by with).

<ex>Mozilla Foundation has finally reached an <t>agreement</t> <fex name="par2">with America Online</fex>.</ex>', 'An expression of the commitment made by the #Parties .



<ex>The US and Russia have <t>agreed</t> <fex name="obl">to share early warning data on missile launches</fex>.</ex>', '#Topic is a description of the domain covered.

<ex>Some types of alloy steel have been covered in the expiring <fex name="top">trade</fex> <t>agreement</t>.</ex>', 'The group of individuals portrayed as equally involved in making an agreement.

<ex><fex name="pars">The US and Russia</fex> have <t>agreed</t> to cooperate in destroying surplus portable anti-aircraft missiles.</ex>', 'As with other frames in the Communication domain, the #Medium of communication may be expressed. #Medium is the physical entity or channel used to record or negotiate the #Obligation. 


<ex>The parties made a <fex name="med">written</fex> <t>agreement</t> to arbitrate disputes arising in connection with the contract.</ex>', 'Any description of the act of making an agreement which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (<ment>quietly, loudly</ment>), and general descriptions comparing events (<ment>the same way</ment>). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of either #Party_1 or #Parties that also affect the action (<ment>presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully</ment>). 

<ex>I <fex name="man">readily</fex> <t>agreed</t> with her to end our wrestling match.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Place where the Agreement occurs.<ex>The town coucil members reached an <t>agreement</t> <fex name="Place">at the City Hall plenary session</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Time where the Agreement occurs.<ex>The town coucil members finally reached an <t>agreement</t> <fex name="Time">at midnight</fex>.</ex>']

descriptions:
['The individual or individuals that form the more prominent (i.e., agentive) party to the compromise, as compared with #Party_2. That is, #Party_1 is generally encoded as the External Argument of verbs, and peripheral FEs that do not make reference to the event itself generally make reference to #Party_1. Thus #Manner is construed to be that of #Party_1, not necessarily that of #Party_2 or the group of #Parties as a whole.

<ex>On Monday, <fex name="par1">the airline</fex> <t>compromised</t> with the Canadian Auto Workers. on a contract for salary and benefits.</ex>', 'The semantically (and grammatically) less prominent participant (or group of participants) in the compromise. It is generally encoded within a prepositional phrase (e.g. headed by <m>with</m>).

<ex>Parenthetical Foundation has finally reached a <t>compromise</t> <fex name="par2">with The Bracket Fund</fex>.</ex>', 'An expression of the commitment made by the #Parties .

<ex>The US and Russia have <t>compromised</t> <fex name="obl">to share a portion of early warning data on missile launches</fex>.</ex>', '#Topic is a description of the domain covered by the compromise.

<ex>They ll never <t>compromise</t> <fex name="Topic">on salary</fex>.</ex>', 'The group of individuals portrayed as equally involved in reaching a compromise.

<ex><fex name="pars">The US and Russia</fex> have <t>compromised</t> on a plan to destroy surplus portable anti-aircraft missiles.</ex>', 'As with other frames in the Communication domain, the #Medium of communication may be expressed. #Medium is the physical entity or channel used to record or negotiate the #Obligation. 

<ex>The parties entered into an <fex name="med">oral</fex> <t>compromise</t> to arbitrate disputes arising in connection with the contract.</ex>', 'Any description of the act of making an agreement which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (<ment>quietly, loudly</ment>), and general descriptions comparing events (<ment>the same way</ment>). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of either #Party_1 or #Parties that also affect the action (<ment>presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully</ment>). 

<ex>I <fex name="man">readily</fex> <t>compromised</t> with her to end our wrestling match.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Place where the compromise occurs.

<ex>The town coucil members reached a <t>compromise</t> <fex name="Place">at the City Hall plenary session</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Place where the compromise occurs.

<ex>The town coucil members finally reached a <t>compromise</t> <fex name="Place">at midnight</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Purpose for which the #Parties make the compromise.

<ex>We re making <t>compromises</t> <fex name="pur">to head off potentially larger conflicts down the road</fex>.</ex>']

descriptions:
['The mass of entities which moves.', 'The place where the overall motion event takes place, thus including Source, Path, and Goal locations.

<ex>Letters <t>streamed</t> in <fex name="Area">all over the country</fex>.</ex>', 'The Source is the location the Theme occupies initially before its change of location. 

<ex>Afterwards, the crowd <t>flooded</t> <fex name="Source">away from the ceremony</fex>. </ex>', 'Path refers to (a part of the) ground the #Mass_theme travels over or to a landmark the #Mass_theme travels by. 

<ex>The door opened , and a crowd of people <t>flooded</t> <fex name="Path">past me</fex>, into the hall.</ex>', 'Goal is the location the #Mass_theme ends up in. 


<ex>Eager spectators <t>crowded</t> <fex name="Goal">into the auditorium</fex></ex>', 'This FE is used for expressions that indicate motion along a line from the deitic center towards a reference point (which may be implicit) that is neither the #Goal of the posture change nor a landmark along the way of the moving part of the body.  Often #Direction is defined with reference to the canonical orientation of the #Mass_theme, or the orientation imposed by an implicit observer.', 'Degree of abundance.', 'When the event occurs.', 'The #Speed is the rate at which motion occurs.', 'The amount of time for which a state holds or a process is ongoing.', 'This FE is any expression which characterizes the extent of motion.  This frame element occurs throughout the motion domain but is very infrequent in Motion.Noise. The engine 	<t>spluttered</t> forward <fex name="Distance">a short way</fex> and stopped.', 'The #Mode_of_transportation expresses how the motion of the #Mass_theme is effected, by its body or by a vehicle which holds and conveys the #Mass_theme. Vehicles can move in any way and in any medium. They are usually expressed obliquely with  in  or  by .

<ex> Fans <t>poured</t> in <fex name="Mode_of_transportation">by boat and train</fex>.</ex>', 'Any description of the motion event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an #Mass_theme that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully)

<ex>Children <t>crowded</t> <fex name="Manner">eagerly</fex> around us</ex>', 'The spatial setting in which the Mass_motion takes place.

<ex><fex name="Place">In the main auditorium</fex> people <t>crowded</t> around Orange s newest computer</ex>', 'In cases wherein the #Mass_theme is construed as a sentient entities, this FE denotes the state of affairs that the entities wish to bring about by mass movement.

<ex>The ducks <t>flocked</t> south <fex name="pur">to mate</fex>.</ex>']

descriptions:
['This is the person (or people) to whom a mental property is attributed. The FE may be expressed as External Argument of a predicative use of the adjective, as a PP Complement, or as the head noun modified by an attributive use of the adjective: 
<ex><fex name="Protagonist">You</fex> were <t>smart</t> to save all your receipts.</ex>
<ex>It was <t>smart</t> <fex name="Protagonist">of you</fex> to save all your receipts.</ex>
<ex>You are a <t>smart</t> <fex name="Protagonist">person</fex> to save your receipts.</ex>', 'The #Behavior refers to any action, utterance, belief, or artifact thereof on the basis of which a mental property is attributed to the #Protagonist. The meaning of the English word <ment>behavior</ment> should not be taken to constrain the identity of this frame element too much---conceptually, this frame element includes things that would not normally be described using the word <ment>behavior</ment>.  Note the following examples:
<ex>That was a <t>stupid</t> <fex name="Behavior">mistake</fex>.</ex>
<ex>The manager made a few <t>stupid</t> <fex name="Behavior">remarks</fex> and then sat down.</ex>
<ex>What a <t>stupid</t> <fex name="Behavior">book</fex>!</ex>
<ex>It s <t>stupid</t> <fex name="Behavior">to think that everything will improve</fex>.</ex>', 'Some of the words in this frame have a slightly different use in that they evaluate someone only with respect to their mental performance in some particular domain or capacity.  We call this domain or capacity <t>Practice</t>. E.g., She is <t>astute</t> <fex name="Practice">at math</fex>. Note that this does not mean she is considered to be astute in general for doing math. Rather, she is astute with respect to math, but may be dumb with respect to other things. These uses probably belong in a different frame with words such as <ment>skilled</ment>, but for the time being they are marked with the FE Practice. General Grammatical Observations:  The adjectives in this frame may exhibit Extraposition of a <ment>to</ment>-marked infinitive VP, with an optional Protagonist expressed in an <ment>of</ment>-PP: It was <t>brilliant</t> (of you) <fex name="Prac">to invite the clowns to the party</fex>.', '#Degree marks the extent to which the #Behavior or #Practice is exhibited or attributed.

<ex>Jenkins is <fex name="Manr">far too</fex> <t>astute</t> to fall for that</ex>
<ex>She was <fex name="Degr">a bit</fex> <t>careless</t> at adding drops.</ex>', '#Manner refers to the method or way in which the Mental_property is attributed to the #Protagonist.

<ex>It was not licence he wanted -- his goal was the <fex name="Manr">forcible</fex> <t>enlightenment</t> of mankind.</ex>', 'The individual whose point of view is taken in assigning the judgment denoted by the target.

<ex><fex name="jud">To my mind</fex>, his dialogues with his Vietnamese counterpart were the most trenchant, <t>astute</t> and screamingly funny things Trudeau has ever done</ex>']

descriptions: ['The #Experiencer is the person or sentient entity that experiences or feels the emotions.', 'The #Topic is the general area in which the emotion occurs.  It indicates a range of possible #Stimulus.', 'The #Stimulus is the person, event, or state of affairs that evokes the emotional response in the #Experiencer.', 'The #State is the abstract noun that describes a more lasting experience by the #Experiencer.', 'The body part, gesture, or other expression of the #Experiencer that reflects his or her emotional state.  They describe a presentation of the experience or emotion denoted by the adjective or noun.', 'The extent to which the #Experiencer s emotion deviates from the norm for the emotion.', 'The #Parameter is a domain in which the #Experiencer experiences the #Stimulus.', 'Any description of the way in which the #Experiencer experiences the #Stimulus which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (<ment>quietly, loudly</ment>), and general descriptions comparing events (<ment>the same way</ment>).  #Manner may also describe a state of the #Experiencer that affects the details of the emotional experience.', 'The Time when the #Experiencer is has a feeling about the #Stimulus.', 'The #Duration is the amount of time for which the #Experiencer is affected by the #Stimulus, #Topic, or #State.']

descriptions:
['The person who uses language in the written or spoken modality to convey a #Message to another person. 

<ex><fex name="Communicator">Her father</fex> never <t>mentioned</t> it.</ex>', 'A proposition or set of propositions that the #Communicator wants the #Addressee to believe or take for granted. 

<ex>A CBS report did <t>mention</t> <fex name="Message">that the First Lady was required to wear a flak jacket for her safety</fex>.</ex>', 'The physical or abstract setting in which the #Message is conveyed. 

<ex><fex name="Medium">The report</fex> also <t>mentioned</t> contamination problems during the manufacture.</ex>', 'The relevant entity or state-of-affairs that the #Communicator mentions.

<ex>If someone <t>mentions</t> <fex name="Specified_content">things like ration books</fex> , my stomach turns.</ex>', 'The #Manner in which the #Communicator communicates.

<ex>Her mother <t>mentioned</t> <fex name="Manner">casually</fex> that Parr had gone to Belfast.</ex>', 'The #Means by which the #Communicator communicates.', 'When the the #Message is mentioned.', 'The location where the #Communicator communicates the #Message.', 'The purpose for which the #Communicator mentions the #Specified_content.']

descriptions:
['The #Force is the body of soldiers that acts in military capacity for the #Possessor. This FE is incorporated in each word in this frame.', 'The #Possessor is the nation, institution, or individual who possesses and/or controls the #Force.

<ex>The American invasion, which drew 26000 American troops against <fex name="pos">General Noriega s</fex> <t>army</t>, was the largest US military operation since Vietnam.</ex>
<ex>Unfortunately, <fex name="pos">They</fex> don t <m>have</m> a very strong <t>army</t>.</ex>', 'A characterization of the #Force.

<ex>And while Serbia is keen to invest in its <fex name="des">dilapidated</fex> <t>armed</t> <t>forces</t>, Montenegro wants to introduce a 4000-strong professional army.</ex>', 'The consituent members of the #Force.

<ex>The US has an entirely <fex name="mem">citizen</fex> <t>army</t>.</ex>', 'The #Domain in which the #Force operates.

<ex>The Persians originally planned to rely solely on their <fex name="dom">land</fex> <t>army</t> to advance beyond Athens.</ex>
<ex>You seem to think that our intelligence and <fex name="dom">covert ops</fex> <t>forces</t> and our military are only able to conduct one mission at a time.</ex>', 'A (permanent or temporary) #Goal of the #Force.

<ex>Right at this time of emergency, the <fex name="goa">revolutionary</fex> <t>army</t> made one attack after another in their rear, thus adding to their confusion.</ex>', 'The length of time that the particular military organization has existed.']

descriptions:
['The #Force carries out the operation. Depending on the size of the operation, this may be as few as one individual or as large as the armed forces of a nation.

<ex><fex name="mil">He</fex> died while <m>carrying out</m> counter-IED <t>operations</t>.</ex>

<ex><fex name="mil">US forces</fex> currently <t>operating</t> in Iraq are due to be recalled within the next several months.</ex>', 'The #Area in which the #Military is conducting operations.', 'The nation, group, or situation which the operation opposes.

<ex><t>Operations</t> <fex name="opp">against Afghanistan</fex> continue to be carried out.</ex>', 'The #Goal of the operation.

<ex>He was captured in a covert <fex name="goa">arrest</fex> <t>operation</t> carried out by an elite group of highly-trained Marines.</ex>', 'The #Possessor controls the army and ultimately directs its goals and methods.', 'The #Time at which the operations are carried out.', 'The length of time over which the operation is carried out.', 'A description of the operation or participants that are not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an #Military that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).', 'The means by which the #Military carries out its operation.', 'The #Domain of the operation, e.g., military, intelligence.

<ex>NATO forces are <m>conducting</m> <fex name="dom">military</fex> <t>operations</t> in highly volatile regions.</ex>', 'The location of the operation.

<ex>This sort of <t>operation</t> should only be <m>conducted</m> <fex name="pla">in open areas</fex>.</ex>']

In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of a #Wrongdoer that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).']

descriptions:
['The #Theme is the entity that changes location. Note that it is not necessarily a self-mover.
<ex>The explosion made <fex name="Thm">me</fex> <t>move</t> in a hurry.</ex>', 'The #Source is the location the #Theme occupies initially before its change of location.

<ex>The policeman <t>moved</t> <fex name="Src">away from the door</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Path refers to (a part of) the ground over which the #Theme travels  or to a landmark by which the #Theme travels.

<ex>Jo <t>moved</t> <fex name="Path">past Dad</fex> into the hall.</ex>', 'The #Goal is the location the #Theme ends up in.

<ex>The car <t>moved</t> <fex name="Goal">into the slow lane</fex>.</ex>', '#Area identifies the setting in which the #Theme s movement takes place without a specified #Path.

<ex>Emily <t>moved</t> restlessly <fex name="Area">around the room</fex>.</ex>', '#Distance is any expression which characterizes the extent of the Motion. 
<ex>The twig <t>floated</t> atop the water <fex name="Dist">for about 100 yards</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE is used for expressions that indicate motion along a line from the deitic center towards a reference point (which may be implicit) that is neither the #Goal of the posture change nor a landmark along the way of the moving part of the body.  Often #Direction is defined with reference to the canonical orientation of the Protagonist, or the orientation imposed by an implicit observer.', 'This FE is used for the #Speed at which the #Theme moves.

<ex>The ash cloud <t>drifted</t> <fex name="Spd">at twenty-five kilometres per hour</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Duration of  time for which the Motion takes place.

<ex>The balloon <t>floated</t> <fex name="Dur">for  hours</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Time when the Motion occurs.

<ex>A radioactive cloud <t>drifted</t> across Britain <fex name="Time">after the fire at the Windscale nuclear factory</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Manner in which the Motion takes place.
<ex>A naval airship <t>drifted</t> <fex name="Manr">wildy</fex> in the stormy sea.</ex>', 'The #Place is the general area in which a specific motion (with #Source, #Path, or #Goal) occurs.']
classificationExplanation: Key FEs: Agent, Theme, Goal; indicates change or interaction; often includes Time/Manner.; Involves topological/kinesthetic primitives: Path, Container, Force.; FEs show spatial/logical relations: Location, Path, Cause; connects entities or events.

descriptions:
['This FE identifies the general #Area in which motion takes place when the motion is understood to be irregular or not to consist of a single, linear path.

<ex>Soot-contaminated snow <t>falls</t> <fex name="Area">in Slovakia s mountainous regions</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Goal is the location the #Theme ends up in (often unintentionally).

<ex>The rivals both <t>fell</t> backwards <fex name="Goal">into empty space</fex>.</ex>', '#Path refers to (a part of) the ground over which the #Theme travels  or to a landmark past which the #Theme travels.

<ex>The boys <t>plummeted</t> <fex name="Path">down the stairs</fex> into the basement.</ex>', 'The #Theme is the entity that changes location.

<ex>In 1977, <fex name="Thm"> Nigel</fex> <t>plunged</t> to his death in his car on a mountain road in southern France.</ex>', 'The #Source is the location the #Theme occupies before the Motion occurs.  <ex>The man <t>toppled</t> <fex name="Src">from the passenger seat</fex> into the road.</ex>', 'The direction of motion of the #Theme.  #Direction characterizes the #Path of motion according to some system of orientation, most often that imposed by gravity.

<ex>I hope your egg does n t <t>fall</t> <fex name="dir">down</fex> .</ex>', '#Distance identifies a phrase that characterizes the extent of motion of the #Theme.

<ex>The student <t>plunged</t> from a road bridge <fex name="Dist">more than 100 feet</fex> to his death.</ex>', '#Duration is  used for the length of time for which the Motion takes place.

<ex>In one storm , 46 inches of rain <t>fell</t> <fex name="Dur">in 24 hours</fex> .</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Manner in which the Motion takes place.

<ex>A few heavy spots of rain <t>fell</t> on the pavement <fex name="Mnr">like coins</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Speed at which the motion occurs.

<ex>Michelle <t>toppled</t> <fex name="Spd">slowly</fex> to the floor.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Time when the Motion occurs.

<ex><fex name="Time">In 1977</fex> Nigel <t>plunged</t> to his death in his car on a mountain road in southern France.</ex>', 'The #Carrier is the means of conveyance of the #Theme.

<ex>Nigel <t>plunged</t> to his death <fex name="Car">in his car</fex> on a mountain road in southern France.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Place where motion specified by #Source, #Path or #Goal occurs.
<ex><fex name="Place">In the kitchen</fex>, the towel <t>dropped</t> onto the floor.</ex>', 'The extent to which the #Theme crosses boundary as it enters a #Goal region or exits a #Source region.

<ex>Sucking in a deep breath, he <t>plunged</t> <fex name="Degree">fully</fex> into the cold water</ex>', 'An event whereby the motion of the #Theme comes about.']

descriptions:
['The #Theme is the entity that changes location.  In sentences containing a #Cause, the #Theme is typically the NP Object.  In other sentences, the #Theme is normally the External Argument.


<ex><fex name="Theme">Pat</fex> <t>crunched</t> over the fresh snow.</ex>', 'The #Source is any expression which implies a definite starting-point of Motion.

<ex>The train <t>rumbled</t> <fex name="Source">out of the station</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Path is any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a #Source nor a #Goal.

<ex>The truck <t>roared</t> <fex name="Path">through the tunnel</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Goal is any expression which tells where the #Theme ends up as a result of the Motion.

<ex>The plates <t>clattered</t> <fex name="Goal">to the floor</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the general #Area in which motion takes place, used particularly if the motion is understood as following a complex or non-linear path.

<ex>The fly <t>buzzed</t> <fex name="Area">about the room</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Cause describes the reason for the motion. <ex>The glasses <t>clinked</t> across the table <fex name="Cause">during the tornado</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Distance is any expression which characterizes the extent of Motion.  This frame element occurs very infrequently with Motion_noise verbs.

<ex>The car <t>wheezed</t> <fex name="Dist">the two miles</fex> into town.</ex>', 'The #Speed is the rate at which the motion occurs.

<ex>My Saab <t>purrs</t> along easily <fex name="Speed">at 80 miles an hour</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Manner is the way in which the Motion takes place.

<ex>The drone <t>buzzed</t> <fex name="Manr">curiously</fex> across the room and vanished behind the table.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Means by which the #Theme moves in the specified way.

<ex>The autochopper <t>whirred</t> along<fex name="Mns"> by supplementing its onboard power with solar generators</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Degree to which the Motion occurs.

<ex>Suddenly , the pole <t>thumped</t> <fex name="Degr">really hard</fex> into an unseen object.</ex>', 'The #Place describes the location of a more specific motion (which has #Source, #Path, #Goal, or #Area).', 'The period of time for which the #Theme moves under accompaniment of noise.

<ex>From there they <t>roared</t> northward <fex name="Duration">for about 10 minutes</fex> before turning up the beach approach onto A1.</ex>', 'The #Time when the motion event takes place.  <ex>The train <t>screeched</t> into the station <fex name="Time">at half past seven</fex></ex>']
classificationExplanation: FEs indicate properties: Value, Extent, Degree; applies to an Entity or Holder.; Key FEs: Agent, Theme, Goal; indicates change or interaction; often includes Time/Manner.; Involves topological/kinesthetic primitives: Path, Container, Force.; FEs show spatial/logical relations: Location, Path, Cause; connects entities or events.

descriptions:
['The amount of time for the movement is ongoing.', 'The #Area is the setting in which the #Theme s movement takes place. 

<ex>Emily rose to her feet and <t>moved</t> restlessly <fex name="Area">around the room</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Cotheme is the second moving object, expressed as a direct object or an oblique.

<ex>Pat <t>accompanied</t> <fex name="Cotheme">me</fex> down the street.</ex>
<ex>The squirrel <t>chased</t> <fex name="Cotheme">after the nut</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Distance is any expression which characterizes the extent of motion.', 'The #Goal is the location the #Theme ends up in.

<ex>The car accelerated and <t>moved</t> <fex name="Goal">into the slow lane</fex>, as he passed.</ex>', 'The #Path refers to (a part of the) ground that the #Theme travels over or to a landmark the #Theme travels past.

<ex>The door opened , and he <t>moved</t> <fex name="Path">past Dad</fex>, into the hall.</ex>', 'The #Source is the location the #Theme initially occupies before its change of location.

<ex>The policeman <t>moved</t> <fex name="Source">away from the door</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Speed is the rate at which motion occurs.

<ex>The car <t>moved</t> along <fex name="Spd">at 55 miles per hour</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Theme is the entity that changes location. 

<ex>The explosion made <fex name="Thm">me</fex> <t>move</t> in a hurry.</ex>', 'The #Mode_of_transportation expresses how the motion of the #Theme is effected, by its body or by a vehicle which holds and conveys the #Theme. Vehicles can move in any way and in any medium. They are usually expressed obliquely with  in  or  by .', 'This FE identifies the #Time when the motion occurs.

<ex>The truck <t>moved</t> <fex name="Time">at 10 o clock</fex>.</ex>']

descriptions: ['This is the being, typically human, that controls the #Vehicle as it moves.', 'This is the means of conveyance controlled by the #Driver. In prepositional phrases, the prepositional object expresses the starting point of motion. descriptions:
['The #Agent is the one who passes the #Theme.  The #Agent is generally realized as the External Argument.

<ex><fex name="Agent">Pat</fex> <t>passed</t> me the ball.</ex>', 'The FE #Theme is generally an NP Object that is passed to the #Recipient.

<ex>Pat <t>passed</t> <fex name="Theme">the ball</fex> to the goalie.</ex>', 'The FE #Source is the starting point of the passing action

<ex>Pat <t>passed</t> the ball <fex name="Source">from behind the defensemen</fex>.</ex>', 'The FE #Path is any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a #Source nor #Goal.

<ex>I <t>passed</t> him the ball <fex name="Path">over the wall</fex>.</ex>', 'The FE #Recipient is the entity which possesses the #Theme as a result of the passing.

<ex>Kim <t>passed</t> the salt <fex name="Recipient">to her mother</fex>.</ex>', 'The FE #Area is used for expressions which describe a general area in which passing takes place when the passing is understood to be irregular or not to consist of a single, linear path. Locative setting adjuncts may also be assigned this frame element.

<ex>The team <t>passed</t> the ball <fex name="Area">around the court</fex>.</ex>', 'A resultant state that the #Theme is in at the end of its motion.


<ex>The center <t>passed</t> the ball <fex name="Result">clear of the double-team</fex>.</ex>', 'A previous state that the #Theme was in before the beginning of its motion.

<ex>Paul <t>passed</t> the ball away <fex name="Initial_state">from the danger of the double-team</fex>.</ex>', 'The FE #Distance is any expression characterizing the extent of motion of the #Theme.

<ex>Pat <t>passed</t> the ball <fex name="Distance">50 meters</fex> to me.</ex>', 'The FE #Means indicates the action that is taken that results in the motion of the #Theme', 'The FE #Manner is any expression which describes a property of the motion of the #Theme which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion. Descriptions of steadiness, grace, and other things count as #Manner expressions.', 'An entity saliently involved in the transmission of force from an #Agent to a #Theme.  It may be the #Agent s bodypart, or some kind of device.

<ex>When playing basketball, you should <t>pass</t> <fex name="Ins">with both hands</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Place is the general area that provides a setting for a specific motion (with #Source, #Path, or #Goal).

<ex>The players <t>passed</t> the ball to each other <fex name="Place">at the park</fex>.</ex>', 'The time at which the passing of the #Theme occurs.']

descriptions:
['The #Agent is the person who acts in a misleading way to create the impression that the #Item is a particular (kind of) #Simulated_entity.

<ex>Most recently, <fex name="agt">Grant Palmer</fex> has <t>palmed</t> himself <t>off</t> as a high-placed "insider" in
the Church.</ex>', 'The #Item is the concrete or abstract entity that the Agent misleadingly represents as a particular (kind of) #Entity.

<ex>From all reports Muhammad <t>passed</t> <fex name="Item">the child</fex> <t>off</t> as his son and behaved accordingly.</ex>', 'The #Simulated_entity is a particular individual or a token of a particular type that the #Item is represented as.

<ex>The colleague who stole your idea and <t>passed</t> it <t>off</t> <fex name="Simulated_entity">as her own</fex>? </ex>', 'The #Audience denotes the people that the #Agent intends to mislead into thinking that the #Item is a particular (kind of) #Simulated_entity.

<ex>Ritchie <t>passed</t> it <t>off</t> <fex name="Audience">to them</fex> as his own original thinking.</ex>', 'Any description of the passing off event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including general descriptions comparing events (the same way) and salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (<ment>deliberately</ment>, <ment>eagerly</ment>).', 'An intentional action performed by the #Agent that contributes to the #Item being perceived as a (particular kind of) #Simulated_entity.', 'An action that the #Agent intends to accomplish by passing the #Item off as a (particular kind of) #Simulated_entity.', 'The #Place is the location that is the setting for the passing off event.

<ex><fex name="Place">In London</fex> she <t>passed</t> herself <t>off</t> as a rich American heiress.</ex>', 'The time at which the #Agent gives the impression that the #Item is the #Simulated_entity.']

descriptions:
['Any expression which implies a definite starting-point of the #Road. In prepositional phrases, the prepositional object expresses the starting point of motion. In particles, the starting point of motion is understood from context. 

<ex>The Way <t>descends</t> <fex name="Source">from the City of the Peak</fex> through a dense forest.</ex>', 'The trajectory that would be followed by one moving on the #Road.

<ex>Valencia <t>angles</t> <fex name="Path">across the number streets</fex>.</ex>', 'Any expression which tells where the fictive mover travelling along a #Road would end up. 

<ex><fex name="Goal">Where</fex> does Interstate 80 <t>enter</t> the city ?</ex>', 'A physical path that a virtual moving entity can be imagined to travel along.
<fex name="Road">The bike trail</fex> <t>zigzagged</t> through the woods.', 'This frame element is used for expressions which describe a general area in which motion takes place when the motion is understood to be irregular and not to consist of a single linear path.  

<ex>The paths <t>crisscrossed</t> <fex name="Area">the desert</fex>.</ex>

Note that this FE should not be used for any locational modifier which would occur just as naturally with a non-motion predicate, like speech.', 'The configuration formed by the entire #Path along the #Road.
<ex>The cavern <t>descended</t> <fex name="PS">in a spiral</fex>.</ex>', 'The direction in which a "fictive" mover would travel along a #Road.

<ex>The pass <t>dips</t> <fex name="dir">down</fex> about 2000 feet, and then <fex name="dir">back up</fex> again.</ex>', 'Any expression which describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion expresses the frame element #Manner. Descriptions of the kind of motion a hypothetical mover would have (including steadiness, grace, and general comparisons) count as #Manner expressions.  

<ex>Drifting clouds lent a mystical touch while the narrow road <t>twisted</t> <fex name="Manner">crazily</fex> through ancient stone villages on the valley floor.</ex>', 'Any expression which characterizes the extent of motion expresses the frame element #Distance. 

<ex>The thin line of dirt and gravel <t>wound</t> <fex name="Distance">another ten miles</fex> and on into the Ozarks before it petered out completely.</ex>', 'An state of affairs involving the #Road that allows the #Road to be in the specified configuration.

<ex>As the Condell Road the route <t>skirts</t> the northside of Limerick <fex name="Means">by passing through Westfield s bird sanctuary on the north bank of the River Shannon</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Speed is the rate at which an imaginary traveler on the #Road would move.

<ex>The road <fex name="Speed">quickly</fex> <t>drops</t> after passing the final turn.</ex>', 'The #Place is where the motion (which is specified by a #Source, #Path or #Goal) is located.

<ex><fex name="Place">In the mountains</fex>, Highway 60 <t>winds</t> and swerves a lot, so we lost a couple of hours from my estimate.</ex>', 'The time when the fictive motion takes place.  This may describe the time when the #Road or a particular piece of the #Road existed, or it may implicitly describe the time when an actual motion event occurred on the #Road.

<ex><fex name="tim">Thousands of years ago</fex>, the Roman roads <t>criss-crossed</t> this landscape.</ex>
<ex><fex name="tim">After it reached the edge of the forest</fex>, the road <t>slanted</t> upwards and grew rough.</ex>']

descriptions:
['The series of interconnected locations that the #Theme traverses.

<ex>In insects, blood is confined to vessels during only a portion of its <fex name="pat"><t>circuit</t></fex> through the body.</ex>', 'The moving object whose motion describes the #Path.

<ex>The thick white line shows <fex name="the">the army s</fex> <t>path</t> from Uzbekistan through Iran, Iraq, Palestine, and into Italy.</ex>', 'The #Source is one of the #Endpoints treated as a source.

<ex>The Cassini spacecraft will travel 3.5 billion kilometers (2.175 billion miles) along its <t>path</t> <fex name="Src">from Earth</fex> to Saturn.</ex>', 'The #Goal is the endpoint of the #Path.

<ex>The Cassini spacecraft will travel 3.5 billion kilometers (2.175 billion miles) along its <t>path</t> from Earth <fex name="Goal">to Saturn</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Endpoints are two (or more) named endpoints of the #Path.

<ex>This Pacific typhoon traveled far inland, knocking down everything in its <t>path</t> <fex name="Endpoints">between Los Angeles and Denver</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Characteristic is a characteristic(s) of the #Path.

<ex>Unlike Hubble, Compton was not designed to be serviced by astronauts in its <fex name="Char">270
mile</fex> <t>orbit</t>.</ex>', 'An entity relative to which the #Theme moves on its #Path .


<ex>Lenny killed at least nine people and caused millions of dollars in damage on
its <t>course</t> <fex name="Landmark">past 16 Caribbean islands</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Direction is the direction or directions the #Path runs.

<ex>There was huge relief that Hurricane Emily s projected <fex name="Dir">westward</fex>
<t>path</t> across the Caribbean is likely to track to the south of oil and gas fields.</ex>', 'A setting location through which the #Path goes.

<ex>Hurrican Dennis was blamed for five deaths in Florida and Georgia -- but was much more
brutal in its <t>path</t> <fex name="gro">across the Caribbean</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Length is the distance the #Path extends.

<ex>The bolt traveled smoothly in its <fex name="Length">short</fex> <t>path</t> through the air to Santa.</ex>']
