descriptions:
['The person (or group) who inspects the #Ground to see that it is safe.

<ex><fex name="pat">Detective Jones</fex> <t>patrolled</t> the northern half of the building.</ex>', 'The area that the #Patrol inspects with to ensure its safety.

<ex>Four soldiers with submachine guns <t>patrolled</t> <fex name="gro">the room</fex>, and an officer made his rounds at frequent intervals.</ex>', 'An entity whose presence would impair the desirability or safety of the #Ground.

<ex>Security guards <t>patrolled</t> <fex name="unw">for intruders</fex> while the game was in progress.</ex>', 'The desired outcome of the patrolling. Normally this constituent is a PP Complement headed by <ment>for</ment>, or a <ment>to</ment>-marked VP Complement.

<ex><t>Patrol</t> all unlit areas <fex name="pur">to ensure no one is hiding there</fex>.</ex>', 'The desirable state of affairs which the #Patrol hopes to ensure by visiting #Ground.

<ex>All our grounds are regularly <t>patrolled</t> <fex name="des">for safety</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE is used for the #Means by which the #Patrol examines  the #Ground.

<ex><t>Policing</t> your neighborhood <fex name="mea">by walking your block and asking about new or unusual things</fex> can significantly improve your and your neighbors  safety.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Manner in which the #Patrol inspects the #Ground.

<ex>My officers have been <fex name="man">bravely</fex> <t>patrolling</t> that estate, on behalf of the community which we serve, and will continue to do so.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the extent  to which the #Patrol examines the #Ground.

<ex> This section is <fex name="deg">thoroughly</fex> <t>policed</t> by the correctional officers every morning and evening.</ex>', '>The #Time when the #Patrol examines the #Ground.

<ex>Officers<t>policed</t> the parade grounds <fex name="tim">during the Memorial day events.</fex> .</ex>', 'An entity used by the #Patrol to scrutinize the #Ground.

<ex>Scouts <t>patrolled</t> the beaches <fex name="inst">with binoculars</fex>, looking for aircraft.</ex>', 'An indication of the members of the patrol.

<ex>We <t>patrolled</t> the area <fex name="mem">with thirty soldiers</fex>.</ex>

<ex><fex name="mem">Our</fex> <t>patrol</t> was ambushed just outside the village.</ex>', 'The length of time of the patrol.

<ex>We ve been <t>patrolling</t> <fex name="dur">for hours</fex> and haven t even seen anyone.</ex>', 'The #Patrol may move around in a #Vehicle.

<ex>We typically <t>patrolled</t> this area <fex name="veh">in heavily armored cars</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Possessor is the organization (usually a nation or group of nations) that orchestrates the patrol.

<ex>The bombmaker was found by a <fex name="pos">NATO</fex> <t>patrol</t>.</ex>']

descriptions: ['The #Perceiver_agentive performs some action in order to have a perceptual experience.', 'The #Phenomenon indicates the entity or phenomenon to which the #Perceiver_agentive directs his or her attention in order to have a perceptual experience.', 'It identifies the part of the body used as a sensory organ of the #Perceiver_agentive.', "The #Direction is used for all path-like expressions that describe how the perceiver's attention is directed during the act of perception, with the exception of those indicating the location of the #Perceiver_agentive (which would be marked #Location_of_perceiver).", 'It is the location, activity, or other characteristic of the #Phenomenon.', 'The #Manner expressions may be of lexicographic interest if they describe properties of active perception.', 'The #Means indicates the tool or method the #Perceiver_agentive is using to actively perceive the #Phenomenon.', 'When the activity of attempting to perceive occurs.', 'Some action that the #Perceiver_agentive is trying to accomplish by directing their own attention.', 'The general location within which the act of perception takes place.', 'It identifies the #Duration of  time for which the Perception takes place.', 'It is the perceptual background against which the #Phenomenon is experienced by the #Perceiver_agentive.', 'The #Obscuring_medium is the medium through which the #Phenomenon makes itself perceivable to the #Perceiver_agentive. descriptions: ['The Experiencer is the being who has a physical experience on some part of his  or her body, or internally. Often information about this frame element is incorporated as a possessive determiner into the constituent expressing the Body Part, in which case it is not tagged separately (from Body Part), as shown below: My legs <t>hurt</t>! Sometimes, however, this frame element is expressed as an External Argument in its own right:  <fex name="Experiencer">I</fex> <t>hurt</t> all over! Here, the phrase <ment>all over</ment> can be considered a kind of generalized Body Part expression, and the pronoun <ment>I</ment> expresses the Experiencer independently. With nouns and adjectives in this frame, the separate expression of the Experiencer is more common: <fex name="Experiencer">I</fex> have a <t>pain</t> in my leg.', 'This FE is the location on the body where the physical experience takes place, typically expressed as External Argument, often as PP complement: <fex name="Body_part">My legs</fex> are a little <t>sore</t>. I m a little <t>sore</t> <fex name="Body_part">in my legs</fex>.', 'Degree to which event occurs', 'Manner of performing an action']

descriptions:
['This FE is the being who has a perceptual experience, not necessarily on purpose. It is typically expressed as an External Argument.', 'This FE is the entity or phenomenon that the perceiver experiences with his or her senses. It is typically expressed as Object with verbs.', 'The #Body_part is the location on the #Perceiver_passive s body where the perceptual experience  takes place.  It is typically expressed in a PP. This Frame Element occurs less frequently than others, because many target words imply that a particular part of the body is involved (e.g. smelling must be done with the nose). However, the verb feel does take this Frame Element, because touch is not localized on the body the same way other modalities are. #Body_part may be expressed in other modalities when it adds specificity to what is implied by the target word in question.', 'This frame element is used for all path-like expressions, except those indicating the location of the perceiver (see above), that describe how the perceiver s attention is directed during the act of perception.
<ex>The detective could easily <t>see</t> <fex name="Direction">into the house</fex>.</ex>
<ex>The detective <t>heard</t> their conversation <fex name="Direction">through the wall</fex>.</ex>', '#State is the location, activity, or other characteristic of the #Phenomenon.

<ex>I <t>saw</t> the baker <fex name="State">baking his special peanut butter pie</fex></ex>.

<ex>I <t>overheard</t> the cat <fex name="State">in the cupboard</fex></ex>.

<ex>I <t>heard</t> fear <fex name="State">in his voice</fex></ex>.', 'Manner expressions may be of lexicographic interest if they describe properties of perception as such: <ment>clearly</ment>, <ment>loudly</ment>, etc.

<ex>Pat <t>heard</t> the shot <fex name="Manner">clearly</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Degree indicates the degree to which the Perception is experienced.

<ex>A nightmare creature, <fex name="Degr">barely</fex> <t>perceived</t> in the swirling dense smoke, was beating at the flames coming from its own body.</ex>
<ex>Older people <t>perceive</t> <fex name="Degr">less well</fex>.</ex>', 'The action whereby the Perceiver perceives.

<ex>He <t>saw</t> it <fex name="Mns">by looking out the window</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Time at which the #Perceiver_passive or the #Body_part perceives the #Phenomenon.', 'The general location within which the act of perception takes place.', 'The #Ground is the perceptual background against which the #Phenomenon is  experienced (or not experienced) by the #Perceiver_passive.  It can be distinguished from locative postnominal modifiers of the #Phenomenon in negative contexts.
<ex>Pat didn t <t>see</t> Kim  <fex name="Ground">behind the bar</fex>.</ex>
In this example, it is implied that Pat looked behind the bar and did not find Kim there.', 'The #Obscuring_medium is the medium through which the #Phenomenon makes itself perceivable to the #Perceiver_passive.  This is similar to the #Direction, but occurs in the opposite direction and implies a movement of the #Phenomenon.

<ex>My mother <t>heard</t> the scream <fex name="Obscuring_medium">through the attic window</fex></ex>.']

descriptions:
['The #Person is the human being. ex:Smithers is a brilliant actor. That actor was terrible. (Note that the Person FE is incorporated in the LU actor.n, so the FE need not be explicitly marked unless an overy Person is named.)', 'The #Origin is the place where the #Person was born or lived a salient part of her or his life.

<ex>Sir Alec Guiness was a <fex name="Ori">British</fex> <t>actor</t> until his death.</ex>', 'The #Persistent_characteristic is a physiological characteristic or personality trait of the #Person which is conceived of as persisting over time.

<ex>Billy Throb is a  <fex name="psc">swarthy</fex> <t>actor</t> who frequently plays in scary movies.</ex>', 'The #Descriptor is a temporary condition of the #Person.

<ex>Hundreds of <fex name="desc">angry</fex> <t>actors</t> were protesting against the new contract</ex>', 'The #Age is the length of time the #Person has been alive.

<ex>I need help with the name of an <fex name="age">old</fex> <t>actor</t></ex>', 'The #Ethnicity is the religious, racial, national, socio-economic or cultural group to which the #Person belongs.

<ex>She s one of the most famous <fex name="eth">black</fex> <t>actresses</t> around today</ex>', 'An expression indicating the context with which the #Person is associated.

<ex>Is she the <t>actress</t> <fex name="coa">from the movie we saw last night</fex>?</ex>', '#Rank is the position in a hierarchy that a #Person occupies within a performance.
 	
<ex>Robin is the <fex name="rnk">lead</fex> <t>actress</t> in La Notte.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the Place where the #Person works.

<ex><t>Actors</t> <fex name="poe">at the Theater on the Square</fex> went on strike</ex>', 'The #Employer gives compensation to an #Person for their work.

<ex>Margaret s fortunes improved in 1949 when she joined a host of young <fex name="emp">MGM</fex> <t>actresses</t></ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Type of performer the #Person is.


<ex>Smithers is a <fex name="typ">stage</fex> <t>actor</t></ex>', 'The #Compensation is the payment that the #Person receives for performing a task.

<ex>Barney Smith is a <fex name="com">highly-paid</fex> <t>actor</t></ex>', 'The #Contract_basis describes the conditions of employment with respect to permanency, hours per time period, or payment basis.

<ex>Smithers is a <fex name="cb">part-time</fex> <t>actor</t> at the National Theater </ex>']

descriptions: ['The #Audience experiences the #Performance.', 'It is the physical entity or channel used by the #Performer to transmit the #Performance to the #Audience.', 'The #Performers generates the #Performance which  the #Audience perceives.', 'The #Performer provides an experience for the #Audience.', 'The music which may be performed in the #Performance, either in isolation or accompanying actions and speech.', 'The usually written directions which instruct the #Performers on what actions to perform, when, and how. It has many different names depending on the #Type of #Performance.', 'It indicates the subtype of #Performance.', 'It identifies one of the possible relations that the #Performer can have to the #Performance. The #Role(s) a #Performer has in the #Performance will determine which parts of the #Script should be performed by the #Performer, as the #Script is written in terms of which #Role (s) each set of instructions applies to. In the fields of theater, movies, ballet, and opera, a #Role generally corresponds to a character in the story of the #Script, while other types of performance may use more general #Roles like lead violinist.', 'The person that performs alongside #Performer2.', 'The person that is also in the #Performance, alongside #Performer1.', 'It identifies the #Time when an event occurs.', 'It identifies the #Place an event occurs.', 'Manner of performing.', 'How long the #Performer is in the #Performance.']

descriptions:
['This FE identifies the entity or substance that is designed to have a particular #Use.

<ex>The first of the two <t><fex name="Artifact">drawings</fex></t> by Parmigianino recently acquired for the National Gallery is a study for the Madonna dal collo lungo.</ex>', 'The use for which the #Artifact is intended.

<ex>The author s lucid text and <fex name="Use">instructional</fex> <t>drawings</t> provide a strong foundation in the basic and functional anatomy of the pain s region.</ex>', 'An individual or individuals that bring the #Artifact into existence.

<ex><fex name="Creator">Italian</fex> <t>paintings</t> include an allegorical work by Garofalo which was mentioned in Vasari  s ` Vite   .</ex>', 'The time at which an #Artifact comes into existence.

<ex>Here s a <fex name="Time_of_creation">1987</fex> <t>photo</t> of how the Union School building had deteriorated after many years of neglect.</ex>', 'The term used to refer to an #Artifact.

<ex>In his <t>painting</t> <fex name="Name">The Fall of Man</fex> Albrecht went through a hard time trying to achieve a geometrical figure of two beings known as man and woman.</ex>', 'An indication of the subtype of #Artifact.

<ex>The <fex name="Type">Impressionist style</fex> <t>paintings</t> of 1896-7 are rare but of great interest</ex>', 'Any indication of what makes up the #Artifact, including components, ingredients, etc.

<ex>The exhibition also features a <fex name="Material">marble</fex> <t>statue</t> of a naked Aphrodite crouching at her bath</ex>', 'The entity--which may be a thing, an action or a state--that is represented by the #Artifact.

<ex>I was able to take a fine <t>photo</t> <fex name="rep">of his arrival</fex>.</ex>', 'A characterization of the state in which the #Represented entity is portrayed.

['This is the person (or other agent) who forcibly seizes control over a vehicle to gain some end.', 'This is the means of transportation which gets under the control of the perpetrator. Example: The terrorists hijacked <fex name="Vehicle">the plane</fex>.', 'This FE describes the people that are suffering as a result of the #Perpetrator s action. 
<ex>The rebels skyjacked a plane <fex name="Victim">with 89 passangers</fex>.</ex>', 'Degree to which event occurs', 'The unlawful seizure of a #Vehicle by a #Perpetrator.', 'Manner of performing an action', 'An act performed by the #Perpetrator by which the seizure of the #Vehicle is accomplished.', 'The amount of time for which a state holds or a process is ongoing.', 'Where the event takes place.', 'When the event occurs.', 'This is the place to which the vehicle is directed.', 'This is the place from which the vehicle is taken away.', 'The action that the #Perpetrator is trying to accomplish by piracy.

descriptions:
['The #Agent is the person (or other force) that causes the #Theme to move.

<ex><fex name="Agent">The waiter</fex> <t>placed</t> the food on the table.</ex>', 'The #Theme is the object that changes location during the Placing.

<ex>The waiter <t>placed</t> <fex name="Theme">the food</fex> on the table.</ex>', 'The FE #Goal is the location where the #Theme ends up. This FE is profiled by words in this frame.  
<ex>The waiter <t>placed</t> the food <fex name="Goal">on the table</fex>.</ex>', '<ex>Grass , which is sown with clover , provides rich pasture for cattle in summer and the clover is <fex name="Cause">another plant which</fex> <t>puts</t> nitrogen into the soil .</ex>', 'The #Source is the initial location of the #Theme, before it changes location. 
<ex>Her mother <t>shelved</t> the cans <fex name="Src">from the countertop</fex>.</ex>', 'Any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a #Source nor a #Goal expresses the frame element #Path. In this frame, #Path expressions almost always have a <ment>via</ment>-sense.

<ex>The mail carrier <t>stuck</t> the letters <fex name="Path">through the slot</fex>.</ex>', 'Any expression which describes a property of the placing action which is not directly related to the trajectory of the #Theme s motion expresses the frame element #Manner. Descriptions of speed, steadiness, grace, means of motion, and other things count as #Manner expressions.

<ex>The chef <t>packaged</t> the food <fex name="Manner">carefully</fex> into take-out containers.</ex>', 'The #Means is an act whereby the #Agent achieves the placing of the #Theme.

<ex>The Egyptians <t>set</t> it in place <fex name="Mns">by simple levering</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Place indicates where the Placing occurs.', '#Time is when the Placing occurs

<ex>Smithers had <t>placed</t> the table there <fex name="Time">the night before</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Area is the setting into which the #Theme is placed.


<ex>She emptied a wash basket full of towels and <t>deposited</t> them <fex name="Area">around the house</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE is any expression which characterizes the extent of motion.  

<ex>I <t>plunged</t> my hands <fex name="Distance">wrist deep</fex> in the fragrant herbs and rubbed the dry fragments between the fingertips</ex>', 'The #Speed is the rate at which Placing occurs.

<ex>Smithers <t>put</t> his book down <fex name="Speed">quickly</fex> and turned around.</ex>']

descriptions: ['The #Theme is the entity that traverses the trajectory.', 'The #Source profiles the region where the #Theme has travelled from (i.e. the region where the #Theme s movement began).', 'The #Path is the region upon which the #Theme travels. descriptions:
['The water in liquid or solid form that falls from the sky is the Precipitation. This FE is incorporated in many LUs in this frame.', 'The area which experiences precipitation.', 'When the event occurs.
<ex>It will probably be <t>raining</t> <fex name="Time">at five o clock</fex>.</ex>', 'The Quantity of Precipitation that falls within a certain Duration.
<ex>It <t>rains</t> <fex name="Rate">only half an inch per year</fex> around here.</ex>', 'The Quantity is the amount of Precipitation that has fallen in a precipitation event or events.  It is usually measured as the height that the Precipitation attains if prevented from further movement.
<ex>It <t>rained</t> <fex name="Quantity">six inches</fex> last night.</ex>', 'The amount of time for which the precipitation event lasts.', 'The way in which the precipitation falls.
<ex>It <t>drizzled</t> <fex name="Manner">softly</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the temperature of the precipitation.
<ex>I was standing in the <fex name="Temperature">cool</fex> <t>drizzle</t>.</ex>']

descriptions:
['The #Speaker makes a prediction.

<ex>The institute <t>forecast</t> an increase in leisure spending of 15 per cent.</ex>', 'This FE marks expressions that indicate an event or state that is predicted to occur or hold at a future time. A single #Eventuality may be expressed discontinuously in a raising construction. This FE is also used for expressions that denote entities with variable attributes about which the #Speaker makes predictions. 

<ex> <t>Predicting</t> <fex name="Eventuality">the weather</fex> is risky business.</ex>
<ex><t>Predicting</t> <fex name="Eventuality">the numbers of disabled elderly</fex> into the future is problematic as it depends upon the projection forward of current patterns of disability .</ex>
<ex> Wang<t>forecasts</t> <fex name="Eventuality">a 9 per cent increase in state revenue</fex></ex>
<ex> <fex name="Eventuality">The European market</fex> is  <t>forecast</t> <fex name="Eventuality">to grow 18.1%</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Medium is the physical entity or channel used by the #Speaker to transmit the prediction.

<ex>The events that would occur over the next several years had been <t>predicted</t> <fex name="med">in the Bible</fex>.</ex>

<ex><fex name="med">Her texts</fex> <t>predict</t> the future.</ex>', 'The #Topic is the subject matter to which the prediction pertains. It is normally expressed as a PP Complement headed by <m>about</m>.', 'This Frame Element marks expressions that denote a set of facts on the basis of which the #Speaker makes the prediction.

<ex>Violence was  <t>predicted</t> <fex name="Evidence">on the basis of disorder at demonstrations in Paris</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Time when the prediction is made.

<ex>John <t>predicted</t> <fex name="Time">last year</fex> that he would be finished by October.</ex>', 'The #Place where the #Speaker makes the prediction.

<ex> Greenman <t>predicted</t> <fex name="Place">at a small dinner party</fex> that the market would rise again.</ex>', '#Manner marks any description of the #Speaker s prediction making  that is not covered by more specific FEs. 

<ex>Smeagol <fex name="Mnr">confidently</fex> <t>predicted</t> that he would win the race.</ex>
<ex>One commentator <fex name="Manner">rashly</fex> <t>predicted</t> that the fad for adaptions wouldn t last much longer</ex>

Note that this FE does not cover modifiers such as  correctly  or  accurately .', 'This frame element marks expressions that indicate forwhat future time an #Eventuality is predicted.

<ex> Cocoa stocks reached a record 1.2m tonnes and a seventh year of surplus is <t>forecast</t> <fex name="Time_of_eventuality">for the year to September 1991</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE indicates the #Accuracy of the prediction.
<ex>Compary president Randall Johnson <t>predicted</t> <fex name="Accuracy">correctly</fex> that a major reworking of infrastructure would be necessary.</ex>
<ex>It is hard to <t>forecast</t> <fex name="Accuracy">with a high degree of certainty</fex> what the weather will be like past two or three days.</ex>', 'An act whereby the #Speaker makes a prediction.

<ex>My regular psychic <t>fortells</t> my future <fex name="mea">by examining the configuration of spent coffee grounds</fex>.</ex>']

descriptions: ['The #Agent is the person performing the intentional act that leads to the preservation.<ex><fex name="Agt">Arlene</fex> <t>pickled</t> the cucumbers.</ex>', '#Medium is the substance in which the #Patient might be  submerged to be preserved. <ex>Tess <t>pickled</t> the beets <fex name="Medium">in vinegar</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Patient is organic matter that undergoes the preservation.<ex>Tess <t>pickled</t> <fex name="Patient">the beets</fex> in vinegar.</ex>', 'The #Instrument with which an an #Agent performs the Preserving.', 'The #Means by which the #Agent preserves the #Patient.<ex>Hannah <t>cured</t> the shrimp <fex name="Means">by injecting them with brine</fex>.</ex><ex>This salami was <t>cured</t> naturally <fex name="Means">in salt</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Manner in which the #Agent preserves the #Patient.<ex>Jake <fex name="Manner">painstakingly</fex> <t>embalmed</t> the next body.</ex>', 'The #Place where the preserving occurs.<ex>Mr. Amber <t>embalmed</t> the woman s body <fex name="Place">in Turkey</fex> in 1922.</ex>', 'The #Time when the #Agent preserves the #Patient.<ex>Mr. Amber <t>embalmed</t> the woman in Turkey <fex name="Time">in 1922</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Purpose for which the #Agent preserves the #Patient.<ex>Jude <t>pickled</t> the onions <fex name="Purpose">to increase their potency</fex>.</ex>', 'The amount of #Time for which the process of Preserving is ongoing.<ex>...oak <t>cured</t> <fex name="Dur">for eight years</fex>.</ex>', 'The extent to which the <fex name="Patient">Patient</fex> is preserved from decaying.  <ex> The corpse had been <t>mummified</t> <fex name="Degree">completely</fex>.</ex>']

['The person who uses language in the written or spoken modality to convey a deceptive Message to the Addressee.
<ex><fex name="Speaker">Politicians</fex> do nothing but <m>tell</m> <t>lies</t> about money.</ex>', 'The person that receives a Message from the Speaker. 
<ex>You re <t>kidding</t> <fex name="Addressee">me</fex>!</ex>
<ex>Satan <t>lies</t> <fex name="Addressee">to everyone, including himself</fex>.</ex>', '#Topic is the entity to which the proposition or propositions  relate, that they are about.

descriptions:
['The locale which serves to confine individuals, generally for the purpose of punishment. This FE is incorporated in each LU in this frame.', 'The Frame Element #Operator marks expressions that indicate which level of government operates the Penal Institution.

<ex>Smithers had to serve time in <fex name="Operator">federal</fex> <t>prison</t>.</ex>', '#Inmates marks expressions that indicate the group of people a Penal Institution is intended to hold.

<ex>Monica served time in the Tennessee <t>Prison</t> <fex name="Inmates">for Women</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE marks expressions that are the #Name of a Penal Institution.

<ex>Kolimov visited the <fex name="Name">Willy C. Peterson</fex> State <t>Prison</t>.</ex>', 'This FE marks expressions that indicate the #Location of the Penal Institution.

<ex>Carruthers spent three years in <fex name="Location">Fort Pulaski</fex> State <t>Prison</t>.</ex>', 'The level of security maintained at the prison.

descriptions:
['The #Attribute is the number of entities that make up the #Set.', 'The group of entities, possibly construed as a collective, whose number changes.

<ex><fex name="set">The union s membership</fex> has <t>dwindled</t> from 1.5 million in 1979 to a little more than 600000 today.</ex>', 'The number of entities by which the #Set increases or decreases.

<ex>Jaffna s student population has <t>dwindled</t> <fex name="Diff">by more than a hundred</fex>.</ex>', 'The number of entities in the #Set afterwards.

<ex>Church of England baptisms have <t>dwindled</t> from 365000 in 1940 <fex name="val2">to 150000 in 1995</fex>.</ex>', 'The initial number of entities in the #Set.

<ex>The number of Medline searches has greatly <t>proliferated</t> <fex name="val1">from 7 million</fex> to 120 million.</ex>', 'A description that presents the #Set s state before the change in the number of entities making it up as an independent predication.


<ex>The human population has <t>dwindled</t> <fex name="inis">from being world dominators</fex> to simple survivors.</ex>', 'A description that presents the #Set s state after  the change in the #Attribute s value as an independent predication.


<ex></ex>', 'Any expression that gives information about points on the number scale that the #Set traverses between the starting and ending point of its movement.

<ex>The top three pulled away from the pack as the laps <t>dwindled</t> <fex name="Path">down</fex>.</ex>', 'The rate of change of the value.


<ex>Those systems proved popular, and they <fex name="Speed">quickly</fex> <t>proliferated</t>.</ex>', 'The #Correlated_variable is an ordered progression of positions or values (most often a sequence of spatial locations) that the (dependent) #Attribute is measured against. The #Correlated_variable is metaphorically mapped onto the progression of time, and in the absence of this FE, changes in the number in the #Set are simply measured against (forward-directional) time.
<ex>Mushrooms and deep moss proliferate as you get deeper into the trail.</ex>', 'Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs,  secondary effects (<ment>quietly</ment>, <ment>loudly</ment>), and general descriptions comparing events (<ment>the same way</ment>).', '#Degree to which event occurs.

<ex>Our supply had <fex name="Degr">completely</fex> <t>dwindled</t> with our war fatalities and we needed a small supply on hand.</ex>', 'The #Time is the time-frame in which the change in number occurs.

<ex>Oh, my aching software: Worms and viruses <t>proliferated</t> <fex name="Time">in 2003</fex>.</ex>', 'The length of time over which the change takes place.


<ex>Cellular phone and paging device ownership by adolescents has <t>proliferated</t> <fex name="Dur">over the past years</fex>.</ex>', 'The value, location, or state-of-affairs that corresponds to the #Initial_number of entities in the #Set.

<ex><fex name="cor1">From California</fex> to Massachusetts, these predators <t>proliferate</t>
uncontrollably and unsustainably.</ex>', 'The value, location, or state-of-affairs that corresponds to the #Final_number in the #Set.

<ex>From California <fex name="cor2">to Massachusetts</fex>, these predators <t>proliferate</t>
uncontrollably and unsustainably.</ex>', 'A location where the #Set changes in number.']

descriptions: ['The entity that acts on behalf of the #Author to release the #Work to the public.', 'The creation of the #Author that is made public by the #Publisher.', 'The person or entity that is responsible for the detailed construction of the content of the #Work.  It is normally expressed in place of the #Publisher FE, but the #Publisher may cooccur with #Author, appearing as an adjunct.', 'The format utilized by the #Author to create the #Work.', 'The date when the #Work is available or is expected to be available.', 'The subset of the population that the #Work is targeted towards or released to.', 'The situation that the #Author or #Publisher hopes to bring about by publishing.', 'The location where the #Publisher releases the work.', 'Any description of the action that clarifies, in a holistic way, the details of how the publication event unfolded, including comparisons to other events ( the same way ) and states of performers of the action that influence the act itself ( peevishly ).', 'The action taken by the #Author or #Publisher that makes the #Work publicly available.']

descriptions:
['The being who has a perceptual experience, generally construed to be not on purpose. 

<ex>When she looked back, <fex name="Perceiver">Maria</fex> <t>glimpsed</t> an arm clawing around the corner.</ex>', 'The entity or phenomenon that the perceiver experiences with his or her senses. It is typically expressed as Object with verbs.

<ex>The waiter <t>glimpsed</t> <fex name="Phenomenon">a fly</fex> disappearing under the surface of the soup.</ex>', 'The #Body_part is the location on the body where the perceptual experience  takes place.  It is typically expressed in a PP.

<ex>Mata Hari turned to her lawyer as <fex name="BodP">her eyes</fex> <t>glimpsed</t> the blindfold.</ex>', 'This frame element is used for all path-like expressions, except those indicating the location of the perceiver (see above), that describe how the perceiver s attention is directed during the act of perception.

<ex>Hard to be certain what I <t>glimpsed</t> <fex name="Direction">through the crack of the door</fex>.</ex>', 'The perceptual background against which the #Phenomenon is  experienced (or not experienced) by the #Perceiver. 

<ex>Had he caught a <t>glimpse</t> of her face <fex name="Ground">among the crowd</fex>?</ex>', 'Any description of properties of perception, such as: <ment>clearly</ment>, <ment>loudly</ment>, etc.

<ex>Pat <fex name="Manner">secretly</fex> <t>glimpsed</t> the designs for a new headquarters.</ex>', 'The #Degree indicates the degree to which the Perception is experienced.

<ex>A nightmare creature, <fex name="Degr">barely</fex> <t>glimpsed</t> in the swirling dense smoke, was beating at the flames coming from its own body.</ex>', 'The action whereby the #Perceiver perceives.

<ex>He just <t>glimpsed</t> the car <fex name="Mns">by craning his neck</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Time, generally construed as punctual, at which the #Perceiver or the #Body_part perceives the #Phenomenon.']

descriptions: ['This is the entity which moves under its own power.', 'Any expression which implies a definite starting-point of motion expresses the frame element #Source. In prepositional phrases, the prepositional object expresses the starting point of motion. In particles, the starting point of motion is understood from context.', 'A relation to a landmark that describes the trajectory in the midst of the motion. descriptions:
['The #Protagonist acts or is apt to act with some degree of consideration regarding the circumstances and possible consequences of an #Action he or she may perform.

<ex>I feel that most do, but typically <fex name="pro">people</fex> <m>are</m> <t>rash</t>, and often reach anger and hostility quickly.</ex>
<ex>I think it <m>was</m> <t>patient</t> <fex name="pro">of me</fex> <fex name="act">to wait another month</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Action is carried out by the #Protagonist with or without regard for the circumstances under which it is performed or consideration of possible consequences.

<ex><fex name="act">What he did</fex> <m>was</m> <t>impetuous</t> and quite irrational - some might say ill-considered.</ex>
<ex>Caryn thinks Tom made a <t>rash</t> <fex name="act">choice</fex> by electing to move to the new camp.</ex>', 'The period of time when the #Protagonist acts with (or without) consideration regarding an #Action.

<ex><m>Were</m> n t you <t>impetuous</t> <fex name="tim">when you were young</fex>?</ex>', 'The extent to which the #Action of the #Protagonist deviates from the expected in its rashness.', 'The individual whose point of view is taken in assigning the judgment denoted by the target.']

['The #Event that occurs at a certain Rate. 

<ex><fex name="Event">Production</fex> <t>rates</t> have risen in the last year.</ex>', 'How much a particular salient quantity involved in an #Event changes over time.

descriptions:
['The individual or body that confirms a Proposal negotiated or created by another party, causing it to become binding in a jurisdiction represented by the #Ratifier.  In the case of direct democratic ratification, the jurisdiction and the #Ratifier are the same.

<ex>If <fex name="rat">we</fex> <t>ratify</t> Management  s plan , we  ll have to take a drug test every week !</ex>', 'The idea or course of action proposed by another individual or body which the #Ratifier endorses and makes binding.

<ex>President Aleksander Kwasniewski <t>ratified</t> <fex name="pro">protocols to the Europol Convention</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Time is when the #Ratifier makes the #Proposal binding.', 'The #Place is where the #Ratifier ratifies the #Proposal.

<ex></ex>', 'Any description of the allowing which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (<ment>quietly, loudly</ment>), and general descriptions comparing events (<ment>the same way</ment>). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of the #Ratifier that also affect the action (<ment>presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully</ment>). 

<ex>It is no coincidence that the EU, on behalf of its 25 member states, has <fex name="man">eagerly</fex>
<t>ratified</t> Rotterdam and other treaties, no doubt seeing it as the perfect opportunity.</ex>', 'An action of the #Ratifier whereby they make binding the #Proposal.

<ex>After Inuit approve the Labrador Inuit Land Claim Agreement, the province will <t>ratify</t>
it <fex name="mea">by submitting it to the provincial cabinet for approval, and then signing it</fex>.</ex>']

descriptions:
['The one who examines a #Text to understand it.', 'The entity that contains linguistic symbols.', 'The idea that an implicit #Text or texts focuses on.

<ex>I love <t>reading</t> <fex name="top">about history</fex> .</ex>', 'Any description of the reading event that implicates details of how the event unfolds or is carried out (e.g. "the same way"); additionally used for descriptions of the #Reader that also affect the event (e.g. "eagerly").', 'An act that allows the #Reader to read in the specified manner.
<ex>I <t>skimmed</t> the book <fex name="Mns">by reading just the section headings and the first sentences after</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Purpose is the reason for which the #Reader reads the #Text.', 'When the reading event takes place.

descriptions:
['The #Arguer wants the #Addressee to entertain the #Content, which can be a proposition to be believed or a course of action to be taken, based on the #Support presented.<ex>Jan <t>argued</t> <fex name="Content">that the evidence was inadmissable</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Arguer presents the #Content before the #Addressee.<ex><fex name="Arguer">Jan</fex> <t>argued</t> that the evidence was inadmissable.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the physical or abstract setting in which the #Content is conveyed.<ex>Jan <t>argued</t> <fex name="Medium">in writing</fex> that the evidence was inadmissable.</ex>', '#Addressee is the entity before whom the #Arguer presents a #Content. <ex>Jan <t>argued</t> <fex name="Addressee">to the court</fex> that the evidence was inadmissable.</ex>', '#Support lends epistemic support to a claim, or provides a reason for a course of action.<ex>Jan <t>argued</t> that gun was inadmissable as evidence <fex name="Support">because the police didn t have a search warrant</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifes the #Manner in which an #Arguer argues.<ex>Jan <t>argued</t> <fex name="Manner">convincingly</fex> that the evidence was inadmissable. </ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Means by which an #Arguer presents #Content to an #Addressee.<ex>Jan <t>argued</t> the evidence was admissable <fex name="Means">by waving the warrant in the air</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Degree to which an arguing event occurs.<ex>Jan <t>argued</t> the defendant s innocence <fex name="Degree">beyond a shadow of a doubt</fex>.</ex>', 'The Time at which an #Arguer makes an argument for the #Content.

<ex> The government <t>argued</t> <fex name="Time">in August</fex> that the prisoners should not be released</ex>', 'This FE identifies the location where an argument is made by an #Arguer.

<ex>The young men will be <t>arguing</t> their policies <fex name="Place">in the school auditorium</fex>.</ex>', 'The period of time for which the #Arguer presents his or her reasoning to the #Addressee.

<ex>He <t>argued</t> <fex name="Duration">for hours</fex> that chess is a sport </ex>']

descriptions:
['The sentient entity that uses language in the written or spoken modality to reassure another person. 
<ex><fex name="Speaker">He</fex> <t>reassured</t> her that she was his favorite ice-skating star. </ex>', 'The #Experiencer receives reassurance from the #Speaker. 
<ex>He tried to <t>reassure</t> <fex name="Exp">his potential customers</fex> that his company not only offered the best service, but also the most competitive prices. </ex>', '<def-root>The <fen>Message</fen> is a proposition or set of propositions that the <fen>Speaker</fen> wants the <fen>Experiencer</fen> to believe in order to feel reassured. 

<ex>How do you <t>reassure</t> them <fex name="Msg">that you really like them</fex>?  </ex></def-root>', 'Situation is the problem plaguing the #Experiencer, about which the #Speaker  reassures them.

<ex>The policeman <t>reassured</t> him <fex name="Situation">about the safety of the neighborhood</fex>. </ex>', 'The physical or abstract setting in which the Message is conveyed.', 'The amount of reassurance given.
<ex>He never really <fex name="Amount_of_reassurance">fully</fex> <t>reassured</t> me about the situation.</ex>', 'The #Manner in which the #Speaker reassures the <fex name="Exp">Experiencer</fex>.', 'The #Means by which the #Speaker reassures the #Experiencer.

<ex>Him mother reassured him <fex name="Mns">with a pat on the back</fex>.</ex>', 'The time at which the reassurance takes place.']

descriptions:
['The individual or group within a society that attempts to overturn the #Current_leadership, and possibly also the #Current_order of governance.

<ex>In 1641 there was a <fex name="reb">Catholic</fex> <t>rebellion</t> against the Protestants of Ulster, and English authority over the island was shaken.</ex>

<ex>In the summer he crushed <fex name="reb">Geoffrey s</fex> <t>rebellion</t> and compelled him to renounce his claim.</ex>', 'The people currently in power, whom the #Rebels attempt to overthrow.  <ex>It was the first time that a man of such high rank had been put to death for <t>rebellion</t> <fex name="curl">against the king</fex>.</ex>', 'The currently-existing system of authority or government.

<ex>At the time they were in <t>rebellion</t> <fex name="curo">against the Catholic government</fex>.</ex>', 'The location of the rebellion.

<ex>It seems probably that <t>rebellion</t> <fex name="pla">in East Anglia</fex> was as rigorously suppressed as in Kent.</ex>', 'The time when the rebellion takes place.

<ex>Did you support the <t>insurrection</t> <fex name="tim">of 1854</fex>?</ex>', 'The means by which the #Rebel rebels against the authorities.

<ex>Those actions virtually guarantee that she will be facing an <fex name="mea">armed</fex> <t>revolt</t> within the week.</ex>', 'The degree to which the #Rebel opposes and fights against the #Current_leadership.

<ex>He was wary of entering into <fex name="deg">complete and utter</fex> <t>rebellion</t>.</ex>', 'A description of the #Rebel or the rebellion not covered by more specific FEs.

<ex>We face an <fex name="man">increasingly violent</fex> <t>insurgency</t> with no end in sight.</ex>', 'The rebellion aims to install a #New_order as their leader. This FE is more frequent with noun targets.']

descriptions:
['The person who acts to come into possession of the #Theme.', 'The person who gives the #Theme to the #Recipient.', 'The entity that changes possession, beginning in the possession of the #Donor and ending up with the #Recipient.', 'The aim of the #Donor, which they believe will be accomplished by giving  the #Theme.

<ex>Rural communities  <t>received</t> government subsidies<fex name="pur">to ensure their continued viability.</fex>.</ex>', 'The act that the #Recipient performs which brings about their reception of the #Theme.

<ex>She received the package <fex name="mea">by signing her name on the delivery certification</fex>.</ex>', 'The time at which the #Recipient comes into possession of the #Theme.

<ex>She received the package <fex name="tim">before the month was out</fex>.</ex>', 'The location at which the Recipient comes into possession of the #Theme.', 'The system whereby the #Theme travels (literally or metaphorically) so that the #Recipient comes into possession of the #Theme.

<ex>He <fex name="man">slyly</fex> <t>received</t> the package without signing for it.</ex>', 'What the #Recipient intends to do with the #Theme.

<ex>James Madison Memorial High School <t>received</t> $1,000 <fex name="pot">to purchase instruments for school orchestra and band programs</fex>. </ex>']

descriptions:
['The #Agent is the sentient entity that creates a permanent record of the #Phenomenon.

<ex>Police informed Mr Wong that <fex name="age">they</fex> <t>recorded</t> the attack as a racial incident.</ex>', 'The #Phenomenon is the entity or situation in the world of which a #Agent becomes aware. 

<ex>Adams took some very good advice and meticulously <t>recorded</t> <fex name="Phen">the events that ensued</fex>.</ex>', 'That feature of the #Entity which is under discussion.


<ex>When Mohammed was sentenced to death in the Anti-Terrorism Court in Rawalpindi
the court <t>recorded</t> <fex name="Attribute">his age</fex> as  17 or 18.</ex>', 'The value of the #Entity that is recorded for the specified #Attribute.

<ex>In the fiscal year 2000, Nepal s imports from China and Tibet were <t>recorded</t> <fex name="Value">at
166.6 million dollars</fex>.</ex>', 'An entity for which the #Agent records the #Value of an #Attribute.

<ex>At the time the Army <t>recorded</t> <fex name="ent">him</fex> as a married resident of the Town of Two Creeks.</ex>', 'Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (<ment>quietly</ment>), and general descriptions comparing events (<ment>the same way</ment>). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an #Agent  that also affect the action (<ment>deliberately</ment>, <ment>eagerly</ment>, <ment>carefully</ment>).', 'This FE identifes the action whereby the #Agent creates a record of the #Phenomenon.', 'This FE identifies the #Time when the #Agent creates the record of the #Phenomenon.', 'The goal that the #Agent has in mind in creating a record of the #Phenomenon.', 'The #Agent uses an #Instrument in order to create a record of the #Phenomenon or to measure an #Entity s #Value for a specified #Attribute.', '#Medium is the physical entity in which the #Agent creates a record of their impression of the #Phenomenon.

<ex>In fact, Pepys <t>recorded</t> everything <fex name="med">in his diary</fex>.</ex>', 'The symbolic system used by the #Agent.

<ex> I <t>recorded</t> my notes <fex name="med">in English</fex>, usually translating from French.</ex>', 'The location where the #Agent sets down the facts.

<ex>From what the book says, these events were <t>recorded</t> <fex name="pla">in the Chamber of Mazarbul</fex> .</ex>']

descriptions:
['The <fex name="Record">Record</fex> is the entity in which events or accounts are permanently recorded.
<ex>She kept a <t><fex name="Record">record</fex></t> of how many times he asked to borrow money.</ex>', 'The #Data is the information which is recorded in the #Record.  It is often expressed as a prepositional phrase.
<ex>Andy kept a <t>log</t> <fex name="Data">of the girls he had dated</fex>.</ex>
<ex>His assistant maintained the <t>records</t> <fex name="Data">of customer payments</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Recorder is the person who records the #Data in the #Record.
<ex><fex name="Recorder">Shirley s</fex> <t>log</t> of her favorite eateries grew every day.</ex>', 'The #Entity is the person or institution for which the #Record is maintained.
<ex>She keeps the <t>books</t> <fex name="Entity">for the advertising firm</fex>.</ex>
<ex><fex name="Entity">The bank s</fex> <t>records</t> were not ready for the unexpected audit.</ex>', 'A characteristic of the #Record.

<ex>Moria s bank kept <fex name="Descriptor">detailed</fex> <t>books</t>.</ex>', 'What a #Record is made of (if applicable).', 'The Medium is the language in which a Record is composed, or the physical medium (radio, ink) in which it is distributed.', 'The region where the #Record is sold, circulated, or otherwise popular', 'The Time at which the #Record was created.', 'The Title of a #Record.', 'Topic is what the #Record is about.', 'The use for which the #Record was produced; its purpose.']

descriptions:
['The    Patient may either be expressed as the External Argument or, as in Health.Cure, may occur as the possessor of the Affliction (an example of FE conflation): 
<ex><fex name="Patient">Pat</fex> is <t>convalescing</t> from surgery.</ex>
<ex>Pat s wound is <t>healing</t>.</ex>
In the second example, <ment>Pat s wound</ment> is the #Body_part, but <ment>Pat s</ment> is also an instantiation of the #Patient.', 'This FE is the injury, pain or disease experienced by the Patient.  Affliction occurs either as an External Argument of verbs or as a PP Complement of nouns or verbs: 
<ex><fex name="Affliction">The wound</fex> is <t>healing</t> well.</ex>
<ex><fex name="Affliction">Pat s wound</fex> is <t>healing</t> well.</ex>
<ex>Pat is <t>recovering</t> <fex name="Affliction">from scarlet fever</fex>.</ex>
<ex>Pat s <t>recovery</t> <fex name="Affliction">from scarlet fever</fex> was very rapid. </ex>', 'Where Body Part is expressed as a separate constituent (rather than in phrases such as <ment>the wound on Pat s arm</ment>), it occurs as an External Argument: 
<ex><fex name="Body_part">Pat s arm</fex> <t>healed</t> up quickly.</ex>
 Body Part is treated as a separate Frame Element although it may be viewed as metonymic for Affliction.', 'Manner of performing an action', 'The extent to which the #Patient s state diverges from their former illness.', 'An act of the #Patient that results in their recovery.
<ex>You will <t>recover</t> more quickly <fex name="Mns">by getting simple bed-rest</fex>.</ex>', 'The time at which the recovery takes place.', 'The location where the recovery takes place.']

descriptions:
['The entity that is in motion, and changes path due to the action of an Agent or due to a Cause.', 'The route that the #Theme was originally headed for.  In cases when the #Theme is an intentional actor or is controlled by an intentional actor, the #Original_path correlates with the (former) plan of the actor.  In other cases, the #Original_path corresponds to what would be expected for the path of the #Theme from general principles or physics.  (In some cases, the #Original_path may indicate the intended destination, and not just the path, of the #Theme.)

<ex>VTA <t>detoured</t> motorists <fex name="ori">from southbound I-880</fex> onto Airport Boulevard to access Coleman Avenue.</ex>', 'The #Path refers to (a part of) the ground over which the #Theme ends up travelling after being diverted.


<ex>Traffic was <t>redirected</t> Tuesday <fex name="Path">along Melrose Avenue</fex> to make way for the scoreboard columns, Mellecker said.</ex>', 'The location the #Theme ends up in after being redirected.


<ex>Not knowing any better, he <t>redirected</t> people <fex name="Goal">to the city center</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE is used for expressions that indicate motion along a line from the current deitic center towards a reference point (which may be implicit) that does not necessarily correspond to the #Goal.  Often #Direction is defined with reference to the canonical orientation of the #Theme, the orientation imposed by an implicit observer, or the inherent orientation of the scene.

<ex>Flight 93 left Newark, NJ, earlier that day, bound for San Francisco, but it was hijacked by four terrorists who <t>diverted</t> it <fex name="dir">back toward Washington, DC</fex> . </ex>', 'An individual that determines the change in course of the #Theme.

<ex>When we ended up over in Marrakech, or Dakar, <fex name="age">they</fex> <t>diverted</t> us up to Tobruk.</ex>', 'An event that brings about the change in the #Path of the #Theme.

<ex>We even got a bonus morning in Tokyo s international airport when <fex name="Cause">a bad storm</fex>
<t>diverted</t> us on the way to Asia.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Time when the redirection of motion occurs.

<ex><fex name="Time">Last Tuesday</fex> they were <t>detouring</t> everybody down onto 5th avenue .</ex>', 'Any description of the redirection which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (<ment>quietly, loudly</ment>), and general descriptions comparing events (<ment>the same way</ment>). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of the #Agent that affect the action (<ment>presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully</ment>). 

<ex>Anything flagged as suspect by this system would be <fex name="Manr">carefully</fex> <t>diverted</t> to another nearby area, remote from public access. and well shielded, for a second screening .</ex>', 'The #Place is the location at which the redirection occurs.

<ex>They <t>redirected</t> us <fex name="">at the corner of Alstone and Bokchoy Avenue</fex>.</ex>', 'The state-of-affairs that the Agent intends to bring about by redirecting the #Theme.

<ex>She was talking about how the passengers <t>diverted</t> the plane in order <fex name="pur">to protect
the nation s capital</fex>.</ex>', 'The method by which the #Agent redirects the #Theme.']

descriptions:
['The sentient entity that effects a change in the #Entity.', 'This FE denotes a complex, abstract, human-created entity with interdependent parts which has either deteriorated or is no longer adequate for its purpose given a change in circumstances and which the #Agent therefore wants to modify.

<ex>Particular attention will be devoted to recent proposals to <t>reform</t> <fex name="ent">UK competition policy</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE is for non-human causes, such as a processes or institutions, responsible for reform.  

<ex>Thus , <fex name="Cause">radical reform</fex> <t>restructures</t> rape offences in ways that it is hoped will facilitate the prosecution process without jeopardising the interests of the accused .</ex>', 'The time at which the change of the #Entity is caused.

<ex>The House of Representatives, on a bipartisan basis, sought to <t>reform</t> the program <fex name="Time">this year</fex>.</ex>', 'The extent to which the #Entity differs from its former state at the end of the reform.

<ex>I am trying to <fex name="deg">completely</fex> <t>overhaul</t> my diet, and i need help.</ex>', 'Any description of the  event causing the change of the #Entity which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (<ment>quietly</ment>), general descriptions comparing events (<ment>the same way</ment>), and salient characteristics of the #Agent that also affect the action (<ment>carefully</ment>).', 'An action that the #Agent intends to accomplish by effecting a change in the #Entity.

<ex>Indeed, as the world becomes increasingly paperless, it would be prudent to consider <t>overhauling</t> the rules <fex name="pur">in order to adapt them to a digitalized society</fex>.</ex>', 'The category of which the #Entity is a member before the #Agent causes a change in its makeup.

<ex>Since 1986, the Vietnamese Government has performed a  doi moi  (renovation)
policy that <t>reformed</t> the economy <fex name="ini">from central planning economy</fex> to market.</ex>', 'The category that the #Entity belongs to after it has undergone the change.

<ex>We have also <t>reformed</t> the office managing system from a "proprietorship" <fex name="Final_category">to a "partnership"</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the steps or actions directly enabling reform.', 'The length of time over which reforming takes place', 'The place where the reformation occurs.']

descriptions:
['The #Cognizer is the one that makes a #Judgment. This role is typically expressed as the External Argument (or in a <ment>by</ment>-PP in passives).

<ex><fex name="Cognizer">The boss</fex> <t>appreciates</t> you for your diligence.</ex>
<ex><fex name="Cognizer">The boss</fex> is very <t>appreciative</t> of my work.</ex>', '#Evaluee is the person or thing that a judgment is made with respect to. With verbs this FE is typically expressed as Object: 
<ex>He <t>regarded</t> <fex name="eva">her</fex> poorly.</ex>', 'A description (from the point of view of the #Cognizer) of the position of the #Evaluee on a scale of approval.

<ex>I hold him in the <fex name="jud">highest</fex> <t>esteem</t>.</ex>', 'Any description of the #Cognizer s judgment which is not covered by more specific FEs, including general descriptions of events (<ment>the same way, presumptuously, secretly</ment>).', 'The #Extent_of_acclaim is a description of the number or locales of the #Cognizers.  <ex>He is <fex name="ext">widely</fex> held in high <t>regard</t>.</ex>']

descriptions: ['The #Agent (most commonly a group of people) is responsible for an attempted rejection of leadership.', 'With words denoting removal (or attempted removal) from office of a leader, the #Old_leader is typically the direct object of verbs, or occurs in a PP Complement with noun targets, frequently headed by the preposition against.', 'With words denoting appointment to a position, the #New_leader is typically the object of verbs.', 'Frequently the #Old_leader or #New_leader is referred to only by his/her role. However, the #Role can be expressed separately from the leader.', 'It identifies the political order that existed before the change of leadership occurs.', 'The #Function is the purpose the #New_leader will fulfill.', 'A group of individuals who are elected or appointed to perform some function of governance.', 'It indicates the #Degree to which an event occurs.', 'It is used for the #Manner of performing an action.', 'It identifies the #Means by which the #Agent attempts to accomplish the change of leadership.', 'When the change takes place.', 'Where the change takes place.', 'The situation that the #Agent attempts to bring about by the change of leadership.']

descriptions:
['The concrete or abstract thing that is brought into an earlier state in which it was more effective and powerful.

<ex>The <t>reinvigoration</t> <fex name="ent">of the tourism</fex> economy is recognised by many as an ambitious but necessary objective.</ex>', 'The #Agent puts the #Entity into a state of renewed vigor.

<ex><fex name="Agt">We</fex> <t>revitalized</t> the Golden Mountaineer Card, and we gave seniors a prescription drug benefit.</ex>', 'An event or state whose occurrence brings about the #Entity s return to a vigorous state.

<ex>Rock has been <t>rejuvenated</t> creatively and commercially <fex name="cau">by hip-hop-inspired rock acts</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the place where the Agent brings about the change in the #Entity s state.


<ex>New York s distinction as America s tax cut leader has <t>revitalized</t> the economy
<fex name="Place">in every corner of the state</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the purpose for which an #Agent brings about the revitalization of the #Entity.

<ex>Older neighborhoods throughout the area should be <t>revitalized</t> <fex name="Purp">in order to be retained as neighborhoods having unique identities</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the time when the #Agent causes the renewal of the #Entity.

<ex>Production was <t>revitalized</t> <fex name="Time">last June</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the action that the #Agent performs in order so as to bring about the reinvigoration of the #Entity.

<ex>Natalie Cole <t>resuscitated</t> her career <fex name="Means">by recording a posthumous duet with her father on his old hit "Unforgettable"</fex>.</ex>', 'Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (<ment>quietly, loudly</ment>), and general descriptions comparing events (<ment>the same way</ment>). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an #Agent that also affect the action (<ment>presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully</ment>). 

<ex>They assessed the effectiveness of four different species of native trees for <fex name="Mnr">rapid</fex> <t>rejuvenation</t> of the mined land.</ex>', 'The device used by the #Agent to help accomplish the action indicated by the target.']

descriptions:
['The concept which is linked to #Concept_2.  

<ex>High death rates among smokers <t>connect</t> <fex name="Concept_1">cigarettes</fex> with disease.</ex>', 'The concept which is tied to #Concept_1.  

<ex>Skid marks on the street <t>connects</t> my neighbor s car <fex name="Concept_2">to my broken mailbox</fex>.</ex>', 'The phenomenon that points to or proves the connection between #Concept_1 and #Concept_2. Generally expressed as an external.

<ex><fex name="Point_of_view">As far as we are concerned</fex> this evidence <t>ties</t> the secretary to the case of the missing apple fritters.</ex>', 'The #Degree of connection between the #Concepts.

<ex>People come from all over the nation to work in and visit the <t>capital</t> <fex name="ContP">of the United States</fex>.</ex>', 'The politically or administratively-defined locale that functions as the head of state for the containing entity.

descriptions:
['The #Captor is the individual who ends the confinement or captivity of the #Theme. The release of the #Theme is in accord with the plans of the #Captor.

<ex><fex name="cap">The police</fex> <t>released</t> the suspect.</ex>', 'The #Theme is the person or object in motion released by the #Captor.

<ex>The police <t>released</t> <fex name="Theme">the suspect</fex>.</ex>', 'The location to which the #Theme s motion was formerly inhibited.

<ex>She petitioned unsuccessfully for their <t>release</t> <fex name="Loc">from Chateau D If</fex>.</ex>', 'Any description of the release which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (<ment>quietly, loudly</ment>), and general descriptions comparing events (<ment>the same way</ment>). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an #Captor that also affect the action (<ment>presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully</ment>). 

<ex>The police <t>released</t> the suspect <fex name="Manner">carefully</fex>.</ex>', 'The action of the #Captor which brings about the release.

<ex>He <t>released</t> a hundred prisoners <fex name="Means">by signing a single document.</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Purpose identifies the purpose for which the #Captor release the #Theme.', 'The location where the #Theme ceases to be confined.

<ex>"Aren t you going to <t>release</t> us <fex name="Place">here</fex>?" she asked.</ex>', 'The #Time identifies when the #Captor release the #Theme .']

descriptions:
['The #Authorities are those who have the official authority to release a #Suspect.

<ex><fex name="Auth">The police</fex> <t>released</t> the suspect.</ex>', 'The #Suspect is the person released by the #Authorities, having been held under suspicion of having committed a crime.

<ex>The police <t>released</t> <fex name="Susp">the suspect</fex>.</ex>', 'The place that the #Suspect is no longer confined to.', 'The #Manner is the way in which the #Authorities release the #Suspect.

<ex>The police <t>released</t> the suspect <fex name="Manner">carefully</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Means identifies the means by which the #Authorities release the #Suspect.', 'The #Purpose identifies the purpose for which the #Authorities release the #Suspect.', 'The #Place identifies the place where the #Authorities release the #Suspect .', 'The #Time identifies when the #Authorities release the #Suspect .']

descriptions:
['The person or thing whose needs are served by the #Means action.

<ex> <fex name="Protagonist">I</fex> <t>rely</t> on this walking stick to get me around</ex>', 'An action or state that the #Means action brings about and from which the #Protagonist profits.

<ex>He still <t>relies</t> on me <fex name="Benefit">to do the head work</fex></ex>', 'The person or thing that performs a #Means action to the #Benefit of the #Protagonist.

<ex>We <t>rely</t>  <fex name="Intermediary">on you</fex> for feedback and input</ex>', 'An object that the #Protagonist uses to realize a #Benefit.

<ex>I hate having to <t>rely</t> <fex name="Instrument">on my car</fex> to get where I want to go</ex>', 'An action or state that the #Protagonist wants to bring about.  It is more or less the #Benefit viewed in relation to the #Protagonist rather than the #Means.

<ex>The user will quickly begin to <t>rely</t> on the pills <fex name="Purpose">to cope with life pressures</fex>.</ex>', 'An action performed either by the #Protagonist or an #Intermediary that has a #Benefit for  the #Protagonist attached to it.


<ex>We <t>rely</t> <fex name="Means">on your response</fex></ex>', 'The extent to which the achievement of the #Benefit depends on the #Means action.

<ex> He knows I <t>rely</t> <fex name="Degree">entirely</fex> on him to point out all my blunders and spelling mistakes.</ex>']

descriptions: ['An individual that considers the Expectation certain.', 'The fact which the #Cognizer depends on in planning for the future.\n\n<ex>I think you should <t>count</t> <fex name="Expectation">on getting a paycut</fex>.</ex>']

descriptions: ['The #Believer is the person whose religious beliefs are in question.', "The #Content is the object of the #Believer's religious conviction.", 'It is a  function that an #Element of the faith is believed to serve.', 'A predefined, fixed idea to which reference is made throughout the belief system.', 'It identifies the #Degree to which the #Believer is convinced of the usefulness and validity of their religious beliefs.', 'It identifies the #Manner in which the #Believer knows or thinks something.']

descriptions:
['The part of the Resource that the Process has not yet applied to.', 'The original set of items to which the Depletive_process applies one after the other.', 'The individual who owned the Resource that is being depleted.

<ex>Only two poems are <t>left</t> <fex name="oo">from Virgil Smithers</fex></ex>', 'A temporal setting or event which metonymically stands for the entities that were in existence then. The #Remainder is part of the #Original_context. This FE occurs especially when abstract entities such as social institutions are seen as #Resources.  <ex>Only the Beaux Arts cinema is <t>left</t> <fex name="oc">from the golden era of the 1940s</fex></ex>', 'The place where the Resource is found.

<ex>How much is <t>left</t> <fex name="loc">in the treasury</fex>?</ex>', 'The Time when the amount of the Resource that is let is assessed.

<ex><fex name="tm">In 1910</fex>, only two of the Renaissance buildings <t>remained</t></ex>']
classificationExplanation: FEs show spatial/logical relations: Location, Path, Cause; connects entities or events.

descriptions:
['A person who recalls memories of past experience or impressions.


<ex>I will truly miss Delmar and <fex name="cog">I</fex> will always <t>remember</t> when his eyes lit up everytime he got ice-cream.</ex>', 'A person or thing that the #Cognizer remembers as being involved in some state or event or as having some property.

<ex>today I saw a sister s exclassmate, and didn t recognise him, I <t>remember</t> <fex name="sal">him</fex> taller.</ex>', 'A past event or state that the #Cognizer witnessed and is now recalling. The #Experience may be a specific event or state or a vaguer period that is treated as a unit.

<ex> I can still <t>remember</t> <fex name="exp">saying the pledge every morning before class in elementary school</fex>, a rare occurrence among elementary school kids nowadays. </ex>', 'A property that the #Cognizer attributes to the #Salient_entity based on their past experience of the #Salient_entity or a category to which the #Salient_entity is assigned.

<ex>I <t>remember</t> Peter <fex name="imp">as immensely kind in those first three weeks before I was packed off to boarding school</fex>.</ex>', 'A property or role that a #Salient_entity actually held and which frames the memories that the #Cognizer has of the #Salient_entity.

<ex>Atulprasad is principally <t>remembered</t> <fex name="sta">as a musician and composer</fex>. </ex>', 'The degree of detail and/or immediacy of  a remembered #Experience.

<ex>Brian Wooley only <fex name="viv">dimly</fex> <t>remembered</t> the hand falling on his shoulder</ex>', 'A situation in which the Cognizer encountered the #Salient_entity.

<ex>She was just how I <t>remember</t> her <fex name="con">from school</fex> .</ex>', 'The time when the #Cognizer remembers the experience.

<ex>I  ll <fex name="tim">never</fex> <t>forget</t> being in hospital</ex>', 'Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the #Cognizer that affect the action (fondly). 

<ex>Bill will be <fex name="man">fondly</fex> <t>remembered</t> by all.</ex>', 'The amount of time for which the #Cognizer remembers the experience.

<ex> Your generosity and kindness will be long <t>remembered</t> <fex name="dur">for years to come</fex>. </ex>']

descriptions:
['The person who retains and retrieves information.

<ex>I bet <fex name="cog">Johno</fex> can <t>remember</t> the name of the Ramones  drummer.</ex>', '#Mental_content stored by and accessible to the #Cognizer. It may derive from experience or activity or simply from being informed of it.

<ex>Do you <t>remember</t> <fex name="men">how to spell cupboard ?</fex></ex>', 'The extent to which the remembered #Mental_content matches the information or experience that it is based on.

<ex>If I <t>remember</t> <fex name="acc">correctly</fex> the pre-War price for hanging a wall up, a roll of wallpaper was about sixpence a roll , and that included preparing as well.</ex>', 'The time when the #Cognizer retrieves #Mental_content or forgets it.

<ex> <fex name="tim">Afterwards</fex>, I <t>forgot</t> what I was pissed off about, but I was. </ex>', 'An entity, concrete or abstract, or an event that the remembered #Mental_content is about.


<ex>The boy who suffered brain damage when he was hit by a vehicle while walking into school testified Friday he <t>remembers</t> nothing <fex name="top">about being hit</fex>. </ex>', 'A situation or a text from which the #Mental_content was acquired.

<ex>You will <t>remember</t> <fex name="con">from grade school</fex> that you do not want to make the nuns impatient.</ex>']
classificationExplanation: FEs show spatial/logical relations: Location, Path, Cause; connects entities or events.

descriptions:
['The person who thinks of or fails to think of an obligatory or desireable #Action.

<ex>.<fex name="cog">Alex and Zach Swindle</fex>, Centre, visited their brother in San Francisco in June and <t>remembered</t> to take along a copy of The Post>. </ex>', 'An #Action that the #Cognizer is supposed to carry out. This may be a Task imposed specifically on the #Cognizer or it may be self- or socially imposed.

<ex>This time we <t>remembered</t> <fex name="act">to turn the gas off before leaving</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Salient_entity is a person or thing that the #Cognizer needs to affect or effect when carrying out the #Action.

<ex>We piled into the taxi and right when we were about to leave, I realized we <t>forgot</t> <fex name="sal">the video camera</fex></ex>', 'A #Time when the #Cognizer remembers or fails to carry out an #Action.


<ex>He <t>remembered</t> <fex name="tim">afterwards</fex> to kiss her fingers.</ex>', 'This FE describes how often the Action is performed by the Cognizer.']
classificationExplanation: FEs show spatial/logical relations: Location, Path, Cause; connects entities or events.

descriptions:
['The #Agent is the person (or other force) that causes the #Theme to move. 

<ex><fex name="Agent">The waiter</fex> <t>removed</t> the dishes from the table.</ex>', 'Theme is the object that changes location. 

<ex>The waiter <t>removed</t> <fex name="Theme">the dishes</fex> from the table. </ex>', 'The #Source is the initial location of the #Theme, before it changes location.

<ex>The waiter <t>removed</t> the dishes <fex name="Source">from the table</fex>.</ex>

A few of the words in this frame participate in an alternation between a #Theme object and a #Source object.

<ex>The waiter <t>cleared</t> <fex name="Thm">the dishes</fex> <fex name="Src">from the table</fex>.</ex>
<ex>The waiter <t>cleared</t> <fex name="Src">the table</fex> <fex name="Thm">of dishes</fex>.</ex>
<ex>The plumber <t>drained</t> <fex name="Thm">the water</fex> <fex name="Src">from the sink</fex>.</ex>
<ex>The plumber <t>drained</t> <fex name="Src"> the sink</fex> <fex name="Thm">of water</fex>.</ex>
<ex>The gardener <t>emptied</t> <fex name="Thm">the soil</fex> <fex name="Src">from the bucket</fex>.</ex>
<ex>The gardener <t>emptied</t> <fex name="Src">the bucket</fex> <fex name="Thm">of soil</fex>.</ex>
<ex>The army <t>evacuated</t> <fex name="Thm">the people</fex> <fex name="Src">from the tow', 'The non-Agentive cause of the removing event.

<ex><fex name="Cause">The raging fire</fex> quickly <t>emptied</t> the square of all people.</ex>
<ex><fex name="Cause">Magnetic storms on the sun</fex> sometimes <t>eject</t> hot gas from beneath the surface.</ex>', 'Any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a #Source nor a #Goal expresses the frame element #Path. In this frame, #Path expressions almost always have a <ment>via</ment>-sense. 

<ex>The army <t>evacuated</t> the townspeople <fex name="Path">through the tunnel</fex>. </ex>', 'The #Goal is the location where the #Theme ends up. This Frame Element is not profiled by words in this frame, though it may occasionally be expressed, as with the word <ment>remove</ment>.

<ex>Grandmother <t>removed</t> the fine china <fex name="Goal">to a safe place</fex>.</ex>', 'Any expression which describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion expresses the frame element #Manner. Descriptions of speed, steadiness, grace, means of motion, and other things count as Manner expressions.

<ex>The army <t>evacuated</t> the townspeople <fex name="Manner">efficiently</fex></ex>', 'The #Means is an act whereby the #Agent achieves the removal.

<ex>You ll have to <t>eject</t> it <fex name="Mns">by sticking an unfolded paperclip into that little hole</fex>.</ex>', 'Degree to which event occurs', 'The #Distance is any expression which characterizes the extent of motion.  This frame element occurs throughout the motion domain but is very infrequent in Motion_Noise. 

<ex>John <t>withdrew</t> the gun <fex name="Distance">a few feet</fex>.</ex>', 'The time at which the removal takes place.

<ex><fex name="Time">That year</fex> they drained the swamp-water out of Higgs  bottomland.</ex>', 'The location where the removal takes place.

<ex>You just take the dishes and <t>rinse</t> them <fex name="pla">in the kitchen</fex>, okay?</ex>', 'The tool the #Agent uses to remove the #Theme from its #Source.', 'To what end the #Agent or #Cause removes the #Theme.', 'This FE is used to indicate the #Direction that the #Theme travels when it is removed.']

descriptions:
['The #Agent is the entity that does the emptying. 
<ex><fex name="Agent">Pat</fex> <t>cleared</t> the table of dishes.</ex>', 'The #Theme, when overtly expressed, invariably occurs in a PP Complement headed by "of".
<ex>Pat <t>cleared</t> the table <fex name="Thm">of dishes</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Source is the region or container which is emptied of something.  In this frame, the #Source occurs as an NP Object. <ex>Pat <t>cleared</t> <fex name="Src">the table</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Cause is the non-sentient force which instigates the emptying. <ex><fex name="Cause">A basic washing</fex> can <t>degrease</t> <fex name="Source">your workclothes</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Path is any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a #Source nor a #Goal.
<ex>Kim <t>emptied</t> the basin <fex name="Path">over the edge of the balcony</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Goal is any expression which tells where the #Theme ends up, or would end up, as a result of the motion.
<ex>The children <t>emptied</t> the toy box <fex name="Goal">onto the floor</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Degree to which the #Agent empties the #Source.', 'The #Manner in which the #Agent empties the #Source.', 'The #Means by which the #Agent empties the #Source.', 'This is the object with which the #Agent empties the #Source.
<ex>I <t>emptied</t> the tub <fex name="Inst">with a bucket</fex>.</ex>
<ex>I <t>peeled</t> the oranges <fex name="Inst">with a knife</fex>.</ex>', 'The location where the emptying takes place.

<ex>Don t <t>eviscerate</t> him <fex name="pla">in the house</fex>!</ex>', 'The time at which the emptying takes place.

<ex>The prisoners were <t>deloused</t> <fex name="tim">in June and August</fex>.</ex>']

descriptions: ['The sentient entity causes the Artifact to not function.', 'The device that no longer works.', 'An inanimate entity or force that renders the #Artifact non-functional.', 'The action that the #Agent performs which results in the #Artifact being inoperable.\n\n<ex>I guess I <t>broke</t> the doorknob <fex name="Mns">by twisting it too hard</fex>.</ex>', 'The time at which the event takes place.', 'The place at which the event takes place.', 'A holistic description of the event (e.g. <ment>the same way</ment>), including depictions of the #Agent in terms of how the state of the #Agent affects the overall action (e.g <ment>maliciously</ment>).', 'This FE is used for any #Depictive phrase describing the state of particpant while lack of functionality is being rendered.']
