descriptions:
['This FE is only expressed with certain nouns in this frame, and then only with the help of a support verb.', 'This is the entity or phenomenon which gives rise to the sensation. In cases of veridical sensation, this FE is not typically distinguished from #Percept. ', 'The FE #Percept is used for phrases that express the characteristic property of a sensation. Typically this characteristic property is described with reference to the entity or phenomenon that produces the sensation---that  is why this FE can be difficult to distinguish from Source. It is possible for Source and Percept to be expressed separately, however: 
<ex><fex name="Source">This herb</fex> <gov>gives off</gov> a <t>smell</t> <fex name="Percept">of garlic</fex>.</ex>
 In cases in which it is impossible to distinguish between Source and Percept, Percept is used as the default FE.', 'This FE is assigned to phrases expressing the body part in which a sensation is located. Since touch is the least localized of sense modalities, words relating to haptic sensations are the ones most likely to occur with this FE: 
<ex>I have a tingling <t>feeling</t> <fex name="Body_part">in my hands</fex>.</ex>
 Words for other sense modalities can occasionally express this FE as well:
<ex>There is a bitter <t>taste</t> <fex name="Body_part">on the back of my tongue</fex>.</ex>
<ex>The ringing <t>sound</t> <fex name="Body_part">in my left ear</fex> distracted me during class. </ex>', 'This FE is the perceptual background against which the Phenomenon is experienced by the Perceiver.', '<def-root>A characterization of the sensation not covered by a more specific FE.</def-root>', 'The #Time when the sensation is perceived.', 'This FE describes how strong the sensation is.']

descriptions:
['The objects being sent.


<ex>Today the <fex name="sen"><t>post</t></fex> contained a pair of brand new tyres from Continental.</ex>', 'This is the person or organization who initiates the movement of the #Sent_item and, unlike in the case of Carrying, does not accompany it.

<ex>Today s <t>mail</t> <fex name="Sender">from eBay</fex> brought me a copy of this book.</ex>', 'The end of the path and intended goal of the sending.

<ex>My <t>shipment</t> <fex name="Goal">to Australia</fex> was received in 10 days.</ex>', 'The beginning of the path, similar to source except that it serves to define the path.

<ex><t>Mail</t> <fex name="Source">from England</fex> to our office is sent registered mail.</ex>', 'The mode of sending employed.

<ex>We promise to expedite your <fex name="tra">air</fex> <t>mail</t> as agreed.</ex>', 'This is the recipient of the sent #Sent_item

<ex>He went to the study and examined <fex name="Recipient">his</fex> mail</ex>', 'The  place in which the Sent_item is located.

<ex>After that, I notice the <t>mail</t> <fex name="Place">on the table</fex>.</ex>']

descriptions:
['The #Convict is given a #Sentence by the #Court.
<ex>Judge Duncan <t>sentenced</t> <fex name="Convict">Marty</fex> to five years under house detention.</ex>', 'The #Court imposes a #Sentence on a #Convict.
<ex><fex name="Court">Judge Duncan</fex> <t>sentenced</t> Marty to five years under house detention.</ex>', 'The #Sentence is imposed on the #Convict by the #Court.
<ex>Judge Duncan <t>sentenced</t> Marty <fex name="Sentence">to five years under house detention</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE denotes the duration of the sentence.
<ex><fex name="Convict">Tom</fex> will recieve a <fex name="Sentence"><t>sentence</t></fex> <fex name="Term_of_sentence">of five years</fex>.</ex>', 'The illegal action of which the #Convict has been found guilty and for which they are punished.

<ex>A pet store owner was <t>sentenced</t> <fex name="off">for illegaly smuggling protected reptiles</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE denotes the type of sentence.

descriptions:
['The #Whole is a single entity or an aggregate of entities which is separated into #Parts.  It is usually the NP Object of a target verb. 
<ex>The directors <t>split</t> <fex name="Whole">the company</fex> in two.</ex>
<ex> The teacher <t>divided</t> <fex name="Whole">the children</fex> into groups. </ex>', 'This refers collectively to the Parts resulting from separation of a Whole:
<ex>The directors <t>split</t> the company <fex name="Parts">in two</fex>.</ex>
<ex>The teacher <t>divided</t> the children <fex name="Parts">into groups</fex>.</ex>
 <ex>The teacher <t>separated</t> the children <fex name="Parts">from each other</fex>. </ex>', 'Part-1 is the first of two Parts mentioned. This is most often the Object of a verb. 
<ex>First, <t>separate</t> <fex name="Part_1">the yolks</fex> from the whites. </ex>', '#Part_2 is the second of two #Parts mentioned. This is usually a PP Complement. First, <ex><t>separate</t> the yolks <fex name="Part_2">from the whites</fex>. </ex>', 'With causative uses of these verbs, an Agent separates or divides something.  The Agent is usually expressed as the External Argument: 
<ex><fex name="Agent">The researcher</fex> <t>divided</t> subjects into groups. </ex>', 'The #Cause is the non-agent that brings about the separation.', 'This frame element expresses a property of the Parts or components of the Whole which is used as the basis for separation. Criterion is typically expressed as a PP headed by <ment>by</ment>, or as a complex PP of the form <ment>on the basis of</ment>.... 
<ex>The researcher <t>divided</t> subjects into groups <fex name="Criterion">on the basis of eye color</fex>.</ex>
As with the frame General.Joining (above), words in this frame can be used statively, as in <ment>The rooms are separated by a partition</ment>, inchoatively, as in <ment>The group separated</ment> or causatively, as in <ment>I separated the laundry</ment>.', 'Manner of performing an action', 'Degree to which event occurs', 'An act of the #Agent that accomplishes the separation.
<ex>You can <t>bisect</t> an angle <fex name="Mns">by drawing three circles</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Time when the separation occurs.
<ex>I <t>divided</t> the prize money among the winners <fex name="Time">that night</fex>.</ex>.', 'Where the separation occurs.
<ex>You should <t>separate</t> the yolks from the whites <fex name="Place">over the sink</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Instrument used to cause the separation.
<ex>I <t>sectioned</t> the block <fex name="Inst">with a knife</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Agent s intent for separating the #Whole.']

descriptions: ['This is the entity which moves under its own power.', 'Any expression which implies a definite starting-point of motion expresses the frame element #Source. In prepositional phrases, the prepositional object expresses the starting point of motion. ['The entity whose rapid, dangerous motion is caused by the Agent and/or Firearm.', 'The Agent is the person performing the intentional act causing the motion of the Projectile.', 'The device used by the Agent to cause the motion of the Projectile.', 'This FE is any expression characterizing the extent of motion of the Theme. Because this concept is associated with finance, the #Purpose is normally to earn a profit.', 'This FE identifies the time at which the #Intermediary engages in short selling.', 'This FE identifies the place at which the short selling occurs.', 'Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). descriptions:
['An entity which is periodically visible from the earth s surface.


<ex>Heku left one morning as <fex name="ast">the sun</fex> <t>rose</t> along the plain.</ex>', 'The #Goal is the location above the horizon that the #Astronomical_entity ends up in.', 'This FE identifies the #Manner in which the motion of the  #Astronomical_entity takes place. It covers any description of the appearance  which is not covered by more specific FEs such as #Speed,  including general descriptions comparing events (<ment>the same way</ment>), and aesthetic judgements of the event .

<ex>The sun <t>rose</t> <fex name="man">beautifully</fex>, but the air was cold.</ex>', 'The sequence of locations that the #Astronomical_entity traverses as it comes over the horizon.

<ex>A pale copper sun <t>rose</t> <fex name="pat">through a sea of muted cloud layers</fex>, and now there is no afterglow at all.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Speed at which the #Astronomical_entity comes into view.


<ex>In the summer, the sun <t>comes up</t> <fex name="spe">quickly</fex>, and you?ll need to work fast. </ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Time when the #Astronomical_entity appears.

<ex>As the sun <t>came</t> <t>up</t> <fex name="tim">at 5:15 am</fex>, it made the red rocks almost glow.</ex>', 'The #Source is the location the #Astronomical_entity is construed to occupy before coming above the horizon.


<ex>The night passed away, and the sun <t>rose</t> <fex name="Src">from the ocean</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Place from which the motion of the #Astronomical_entity is viewed.

<ex>The very end of the eclipse will be visible as the sun <t>rises</t> <fex name="pla">in some locations in Africa and northeastern North America</fex>.</ex>', 'The extent to which the #Astronomical_entity comes into view above the horizon.

<ex>When the sun <t>rose</t> <fex name="deg">fully</fex>, painting the area a beautiful red gold his father would turn to him and smile.</ex>']

descriptions:
['The signatory of the #Agreement.

<ex><fex name="sig1">Eisenhower</fex> <t>signed</t> a treaty  with aliens.</ex>', 'The entity signifying the identity of the #Signatory, resulting from the act of writing on the #Agreement document.

<ex>The Attorney General seemingly never <m>put</m> his <t><fex name="sig">signature</fex></t> on the agreement.</ex>', 'The document which encodes the commitment of the #Signatory.

<ex>I <t>signed</t> <fex name="agr">the contract</fex> and now the dealership claims they made a mistake, quoted the price too low and now I have to pay them more money. </ex>', 'An entity directed by the #Signatory that interacts with the #Agreement document so as to produce the #Signature.

<ex>He <t>signed</t> the contract <fex name="ins">with a mixture of his own blood and spit</fex>.</ex>', 'The place where the signing occurs.', 'Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (<ment>probably</ment>, <ment>presumably</ment>), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may indicate salient characteristics of the #Signatory that also affect the action (<ment>happily</ment>, <ment>eagerly</ment>). 

<ex>Bob <fex name="man">eagerly</fex> <t>signed</t> the contract.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the action whereby the #Signatory achieves the signing of the #Agreement.


<ex>He <t>signed</t> the contract <fex name="mea">by writing his name, Cornelis Wynkoop</fex>.</ex>', 'The purpose for which the #Signatory commits to the #Agreement.

<ex><fex name="pur">In order to become a Party</fex> Australia will need to <t>sign</t> the Protocol by 31 March 1998 and then take the binding step of ratification.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Time at which signature is given.']

<ex>John <t>quieted</t> the crowd <fex name="Purpose">to reassert his authority</fex>.</ex>', 'When the event occurs.', 'An act whereby the #Agent silences the #Speaker.', 'This is the subject matter to which the Message pertains. descriptions:
['This FE marks constituents that express the set of objects or types whose similarity is at issue. 

<ex> <fex name="Entities">The two painters</fex>  <m>were</m> <t>alike</t> in being unable to draw acceptably . </ex>
<ex>Agbenugba confirms the <t>resemblances</t> <fex name="Entities">between his own experience and that of his protagonist</fex>.</ex>', 'When there is an asymmetry, #Entity_1 is the entity characterized by its similarity to #Entity_2, whose characteristics are assumed to be known.  #Entity_1 is often an external argument.

<ex>In the first phase <fex name="Entity_1">Barthes s approach</fex> <m>has</m> <t>similarities</t> to that of Levi-Strauss.</ex>
<ex><fex name="Entity_1">Our economy</fex> <m>is</m> <t>like</t> a healthy plant after a long drought</ex>
<ex>I found a <t>similar</t> <fex name="ent1">passage</fex> in Tolkien.</ex>', 'When the #Entities are expressed separately, #Entity_2 is the one whose characteristics are assumed to be known; it serves as a basis for establishing characteristics of #Entity_1.

<ex>The results of Method 2 <m>show </m>remarkable <t>similarity</t> <fex name="Entity_2">to those obtained by Method 1</fex></ex>
<ex>Leadbelly s recordings are not <t>dissimilar</t> <fex name="Entity_2">to those of Jimmie Rodgers</fex>.</ex>
<ex>This is a problem <t>different</t> <fex name="ent2">from meltdown</fex> only in degree.</ex>
<ex>Here we see a <t>similar</t> case. <fex name="ent2">DNI</fex></ex>', 'This FE marks constituents which express a property in respect to which the similarity of the entities is assessed.

<ex>The disc announced Friday is <fex name="Dimension">physically</fex> <t>similar</t> to current music CDs </ex>', 'A fact about #Entity_1 or the #Entities that reveals how #Entity_1 is the same or different from other entities.  (Note the contrast with  as to .)

<ex>His presidency was <t>different</t> <fex name="Differentiating_fact">in that it offered a way forward for the common man</fex> .</ex>
<ex>Banks primarily <t>differ</t> <fex name="Differentiating_fact">in offering different rates for different risks</fex> .</ex>
<ex>We are all <t>similar</t> <fex name="Differentiating_fact">in having two arms , two legs , a mind , and a heart to feel with</fex> .</ex>', 'The extent to which entities are similar to each other, in general or with respect to some #Dimension(s)

descriptions:
['The person who is influenced by the #Situation or #Entity.


<ex>George Gilder would run with this idea later in writing the New Testament of supply-side economics that <t>influenced</t> <fex name="cog">Reagan</fex>. </ex>', 'A state of affairs--- which can be an event, state or conditions---that has an influence on the #Cognizer.


<ex><fex name="sit">The balmy temperatures</fex> <t>inspired</t> me to write a poem on the advent of spring.</ex>
<ex>Hecker said that although <fex name="sit">the sheer age of the building</fex> <t>inspired</t> him to preserve its heritage aspects he also designed its interiors around the ·clean and fresh· mood of Queensland.</ex>', 'A person or thing that has an influence on the #Cognizer.

<ex><fex name="ent">The painting</fex> <t>inspired</t> me to take a risk and use the intense green for the sky.</ex>', 'An Action that the #Cognizer decides to carry out as a consequence of the influence of a #Situation or #Entity.


<ex>What factors <t>influenced</t> Nixon <fex name="act">to issue the doctrine</fex>?</ex>', 'A #Behavior that the #Cognizer is engaged in independently but whose execution is subsequently influenced by the #Situation or #Entity.

<ex>Attention will also be paid to St. Paul , what <t>influenced</t> him <fex name="beh">in his thinking</fex> and what he wrote in his letters about sex, sexuality, men and women</ex>', 'An entity that stands metonymically for the actions and considerations that produced it.

<ex>Does anyone know what <t>inspired</t> <fex name="pro">this song</fex>? </ex>', 'The #Agent acts to influence a #Cognizer.', 'The #Time at which the Subjective_influencing event occurs.', 'The location in which one has influence.

<ex><fex name="Entity">Chavez</fex> has much influence <fex name="Place">in South America</fex>.</ex>', 'The field in which one maintains influence.

<ex><fex name="Entity">Chavez</fex> has much influence <fex name="dom">in South American politics</fex>.</ex>']

<ex>Once you have <t>submitted</t> <fex name="doc">the form</fex>, a request to make an oral statement will be
generated based on the information you supplied.</ex>', 'The #Place where the #Submittor gives the #Documents to the #Authority.', 'The time period during which the #Submittor gives the #Documents to the #Authority.', 'The #Purpose for which the #Submittor gives the #Documents to the #Authority.', 'The #Means by which the #Submittor gives the #Documents to the #Authority.', 'The #Manner is the manner in which the #Submittor gives the #Documents to the #Authority.

descriptions:
['An event or state_of_Affairs that successfully impedes an #Agent or a State_of_affairs.

<ex><fex name="cau">This failure</fex> <t>undermines</t> governmental authority</ex>', 'A person who intentionally or not acts in such a way that an #Agent or State_of_affairs is impeded.

<ex>Tory rebels queued to warn that <fex name="cou">they</fex> would<t>sabotage</t> efforts to put the treaty through Parliament .</ex>', 'A person who is intentionally engaged in some #Activity and who is impeded in that #Activity.

<ex>If he is really <t>undermining</t> <fex name="age">me</fex>, then he is a fine friend.</ex>', 'An #Activity that is being impeded.', 'A state or event that is ongoing and whose continuance or unfolding is impeded.


<ex>Whenever I try to quit they all try to <t>sabotage</t> <fex name="soa">my efforts</fex>.</ex>', 'The extent to which the #Cause or #Counter_actor impede the #Agent or the #State_of_affairs.

<ex>The so-called advantage of a Premier being able to determine an election date is
<fex name="deg">totally</fex> <t>undermined</t> by speculation surrounding the date for the next election.</ex>', 'The state-of-affairs that the #Counter_actor hopes will result from his undermining.', 'The location where the #Counter_actor subverts an #Activity.', 'The time at which the #Counter_actor or #Cause works agains the #Agent.', 'Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (<ment>quietly, loudly</ment>), and general descriptions comparing events (<ment>the same way</ment>). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of a #Counter_actor that also affect the action (<ment>presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully</ment>).', 'An action performed by the #Counter_actor or an event related to the #Cause that enables the subversion.

<ex>They <t>sabotaged</t> our plans <fex name="mea">by cutting off communications between the island and the mainland</fex>.</ex>']

descriptions:
['The #Agent makes an attempt to achieve a #Goal.', 'The #Goal is what the #Agent attempts to achieve.

<ex>If you don t <t>succeed</t> <fex name="goa">in your subterfuge</fex>, then report back for further orders.</ex>', 'A participant function in a particular event or in events of a particular kind.

<ex>Benedikt was <t>successful</t> <fex name="rol">as a master brewer of beer</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Means by which the #Goal is achieved (or not).', 'This FE identifies the #Degree to which an attempt occurs.

<ex>Your attempt at convincing me has <fex name="deg">utterly</fex> <t>failed</t>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Time when the attempt succeeds or fails.', 'The place where an attempt succeeds or fails.

<ex><fex name="Place">On the bridge</fex> he <t>succeeded</t> in putting the wig on the corpse.</ex>']

descriptions:
['The action that the agent undertakes to achieve the #Goal.

<ex><fex name="mea">His daring, almost reckless, move</fex> <t>succeeded</t> and he lived to fight another day.</ex>', 'The #Goal is what the agent attempts to achieve by performing the #Means action.', 'An entity directed by the agent as part of the #Means action in order to accomplish the #Goal.

<ex> All of my usual source books seemed incomplete, but <fex name="ins">your website</fex> <t>did</t> <t>the</t> <t>trick</t>!</ex>', 'A participant function of an #Instrument or #Means in a particular event or in events of a particular kind.

<ex>The machine was <t>successful</t> <fex name="rol">as a reconnaissance vehicle</fex>.</ex>', 'The place where #Means action succeeds or fails.

<ex>A similar strategy, perhaps even bloodier, <t>did</t> <t>the</t> <t>trick</t> <fex name="pla">in El Salvador</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Time when the attempt succeeds or fails

<ex> But it <t>did</t> <t>the</t> <t>trick</t> <fex name="tim">at the time</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Protagonist is the deprofiled individual who makes an attempt to achieve a #Goal by performing the #Means action, which may involve using an #Instrument.

<ex>Download also <t>failed</t> <fex name="pro">for me</fex> on both March and May issues. </ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Degree to which an attempt involving a particular #Means or #Instrument succeeds.

<ex> A 1000 pf cap <fex name="Degree">almost completely</fex> <t>did</t> <t>the</t> <t>trick</t> so I went bigger with what happened to be handy</ex>', 'It denotes an event or state of affairs that occurs or holds at a time that includes the time during which the success or failure reported by the target occurs and of which the success or failure is taken to be a part.']

descriptions:
['The person who uses language in the written or spoken modality to convey a #Message to another person. 

<ex><fex name="Communicator">Nobody</fex> seems to be able to <t>get</t> the idea <t>through</t> to her that she needs a job!!!!</ex>', 'The #Addressee receives a #Message from the #Communicator. 



<ex>According to Masztalics, a good teacher uses experiments to <t>get</t> the point <t>across</t>
<fex name="Add">to the class</fex>.</ex>', '#Message A proposition or set of propositions that the #Communicator wants the #Addressee to believe or take for granted. 


<ex>He tried to <t>get</t> <t>through</t> to his wife <fex name="Msg">that the debt he s taken on buying stocks poses no risk</fex>.</ex>', 'The physical or abstract setting in which the #Message is conveyed. 

<ex><fex name="Medium">The article</fex> <t>conveys</t> Mr. Handley s disrespect for those who disagree with him.</ex>', 'The #Manner in which the #Communicator communicates.

<ex>Rais <fex name="Manner">carefully</fex> <t>conveys</t> his own personal faith journey while respecting those who may have distinctly different views or experiences.</ex>', 'The #Means by which the #Communicator communicates.

<ex>She finally <t>got</t> her point <t>across</t> <fex name="Mns">by miming a shark s fin with her hands over her head</fex>. </ex>', 'The time at which the communication takes place.

<ex>Greenspan <t>got</t> his point <t>across</t> <fex name="Time">yesterday</fex> that a measured pace of rate hikes is still the plan.</ex>', 'The degree to which the #Communicator s efforts are effective at making the #Addressee understand and appreciate the #Message.

<ex>The ads <fex name="ade">clearly</fex> <t>got</t> <t>across</t> the need to respect the health of non-smokers.</ex>']

descriptions:
['The entity whose scalar property is specified.', 'The #Scale is the attribute of the #Item that is being described.<ex>It was <fex name="Scale">hot</fex> <t>enough</t> to ignite the iron.</ex><ex>The workforce is <t>sufficient</t> <fex name="Scale">in number and proficiency</fex> to finish the building in minimum time.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Enabled_situation by which the critical value is judged.
<ex>Can He make a rock <t>too</t> heavy <fex name="Enabled_situation">for Him to lift</fex>?</ex>
<ex>There s almost <t>enough</t> <fex name="Enabled_situation">for thirty people</fex>.</ex>
<ex>That information <t>sufficed</t> <fex name="Enabled_situation">to convince me</fex>.</ex>
<ex>Finding a parking spot is <t>sufficiently</t> difficult <fex name="Enabled_situation">that you d do better to walk</fex>.</ex>
<ex>This legislation was <t>inadequate</t> <fex name="Enabled_situation">in that it allowed many obvious delinquents to escape prosecution</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Degree frame element indicates a modification of the value indicated by the target.']

descriptions:
['This #Victim is the being or entity that is injured by the #Assailant s suicide attack.', 'The person (or other self-directed entity) that is attempting physical harm to the #Victim.', 'An entity used by the #Assailant to cause damage to the #Victim.

<ex>The <t>suicide attack</t> <fex name="Wep">with a car</fex> killed over a dozen people.</ex>', 'Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an #Assailant that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).', 'An intentional action performed by the #Assailant that accomplishes the assault.', 'This FE identifies the place where the #Assailant makes an assault on the #Victim.

<ex>Last week s <t>suicide bombing</t> <fex name="Place">outside the city</fex> was the first in weeks.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the purpose for which an #Assailant attacks the #Victim.

<ex>They continue to <t>suicide-attack</t> throughout the city <fex name="Purp">to intimidate and terrorize the population</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the time when the #Assailant attacks the #Victim.

<ex><fex name="Time">Last Tuesday s</fex> <t>suicide attack</t> killed seven and wounded 20.</ex>', 'The starting-point of the motion of the #Assailant or projectiles of the #Assailant as they move toward the the #Place that the #Victim is occupying in the attack.  In prepositional phrases, the prepositional object expresses the starting point of motion.  With particles, the starting point of motion is understood from context.', 'The #Path refers to (a part of) the ground over which the #Assailant travels.']

['The #Supporter acts to benefit the #Supported by providing additional strength or stability.', 'The #Supported is given strength through the assistance of the #Supporter.', 'The measure to which the #Supporter s assistance brings the #Supported added strength and support.

<ex>The physical therapist must <fex name="Degree">totally</fex> <t>support</t> tthe patient during the exercises.</ex>', 'The time period during which the #Supporter supports the #Supported.

<ex><fex name="Duration">For some seventy years</fex>, this judicial fabrication <t>buttressed</t> racial segregation.</ex>', 'An entity used by the #Supporter in service of the #Supported s strength by support.

<ex>The troops used <fex name="Instrument">their shoulders</fex> to <t>support</t> the doors of the keep, as the enemy forces tried to break through.</ex>

<ex>For years, patrons of the organization have <t>supported</t> our bi-weekly tea-times <fex name="Instrument">with their generous donations</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Manner in which a #Supporter supports the #Supported. 

<ex>The British Government <fex name="Manner">happily</fex> <t>buttressed</t> Saddam s appalling oppression of his people</ex>.', 'An action that the #Supporter undertakes in service of the #Supported s strengthening.

<ex>The foundation <t>supports</t> the building <fex name="Means">by distributing the load to the ground below.</fex></ex>', 'This FE identifies the #Place where the support occurs.

<ex><fex name="Place">In England</fex>, economic incentives are <t>buttressed</t> by closing certain overdoctored areas from new practices.</ex>', 'This FE identifies when the support occurs.

<ex><fex name="Time">Early in the twentieth century</fex>, such economic arguments <t>buttressed</t> the creation of a National Park Service.</ex>', 'The situation that the #Supporter wishes to bring about by supporting the #Supported. 

<ex>The porch tower of Saint-Germain-des-Prés is sturdily <t>buttressed</t> <fex name="Purpose">to support the weight of its massive bells</fex></ex>.']

descriptions:
['The concrete or abstract entity that undergoes a change.


<ex>How pleasing to see that <fex name="ent">Martha</fex> has <t>changed</t> now.</ex>', 'The type of #Entity that results from the change.', 'The category that the #Entity belongs to before it undergoes the change.

<ex>The viccar <t>changed</t> <fex name="inic">from a professional clergyman</fex> into an anti-ecclesiastical activist.</ex>', '<def-root>The value of the <fen>Attribute</fen> before the change takes place.

<ex>At that time, my asthma <t>changed</t> <fex name="iniq">from very mild</fex> to severe.</ex>
</def-root>', '<def-root>The quality of the <fen>Attribute</fen> that results from the change.


<ex>Its color <t>changed</t> from red <fex name="finq">to blue</fex>.</ex></def-root>', 'A characteristic of the #Entity for which a value can be assigned or determined by calculation, measurement , or observation. 


<ex>Test to check if <fex name="att">the color</fex> <t>changes</t> when you place your mouse over the JavaScript image</ex>', 'The #Initial_value and #Final_value taken together as the range through which the #Entity changes.

The main faith of the church has <t>changed</t> <fex name="val">between Orthodox, Protestant and Greek Catholic</fex> several times.', 'The #Entity's situation that results from the change. 

<ex>Tests <t>went</t> from taking three hours <fex name="Final_situation">to needing less than one</fex> .</ex>', 'The situation that the #Entity began in.

<ex>She slowly <t>shifted</t> <fex name="Initial_situation">from giving him makework</fex> to actually finding he had some skills .</ex>', 'The time at which the #Entity undergoes the transformation.  <ex><fex name="tim">Then</fex> she <t>turned</t> into a lizard.</ex>', 'Any description of the Change event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). 

<ex>He also identified all of the places where cancer rates or pollution levels <t>changed</t> <fex name="man">quickly</fex> over short periods of time.</ex>', 'The extent to which the initial and final values or categories differ from each other.

<ex>Industrial economics has <t>changed</t> <fex name="deg">a lot</fex> during the last two decades</ex>', 'An event or state that causes the change to take place.

<ex>Doctor?s practices have <t>changed</t> <fex name="cau">due to concerns over medical litigation</fex>.</ex>', 'fe_place_undergo_change', 'The location that the change takes place.']

descriptions:
['An entity that is construed as a single point source emitting sound.

<ex>He woke up because of the <t>cries</t> <fex name="Sound_source">of the crows outside</fex>.</ex>', 'The place from which the sound emanates.

<ex>There followed a small silence that was interrupted by a distant <t>cries</t> of caged birds <fex name="Location_of_sound_source">from the interior</fex>.</ex>', 'The location at which sound is produced. This FE differs from #Location_of_sound_source in that it does not highlight the sound s spreading from its source to the perceiver s location, which is usually indicated by the preposition "from".

<ex>The <t>hiss</t> of snakes <fex name="Place">outside the tent</fex> was a constant source of worry.</ex>', 'Certain properties of the sound relating to its production, loudness, etc  may be of lexicographic interest if they describe properties of a sounds as such: loudly, shrilly, etc.

<ex>The Brigadier had heard the <fex name="Manner">faint</fex> <t>squeak</t> of rats.</ex>', 'Expressions that indicate the loudness of the sound.', 'The #Path along which the sound travels away from the #Sound_source.

<ex>The books he wrote later tell how he never forgot the <t>howl</t> of the wolf <fex name="Path">across the icy field of Otsego</fex> on cold winter nights.</ex>']

descriptions:
['This is the person who is characterized as talkative, reticent etc.  It is usually the External Argument: 
<ex><fex name="Spkr">Pat</fex> has been <t>silent</t> on this issue.</ex>', '#Text is rarely expressed because the quantity of information given is the focus of the frame, rather than the information content. Texts can occur as NP objects, but cannot be either direct quotes or complement clauses.

<ex>Kim gave a hesitant, <t>reticent</t> <fex name="Text">account</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE is the #Topic on which much or little information is communicated. #Topic is far more frequent than Message in this frame. 
<ex>Kim is very <t>talkative</t> <fex name="Top">about her family and childhood</fex>.</ex>', 'The #Company in which the #Speaker exhibits a certain communicative behavior.  In this frame, #Company occurs in PP Complements headed by <ment>with</ment>:

['The #Agent is assigned to a #Post to perform a task involving a #Salient_entity.', 'The #Post is a location or station assigned to the #Agent where a task or tasks are to be performed.

<ex>You ll be <t>working</t> <fex name="sal">the phones</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the place where the #Agent staffs the #Post.

<ex><t>Working</t> the front desk <fex name="Place">at a real estate office</fex> is not as fulfilling as it might seem.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the purpose for which the #Agent works at the #Post.

<ex>We will be <t>staffing</t> a booth <fex name="Purpose">to let everyone know about our upcoming events.</fex></ex>', 'This FE identifies the time when the #Agent works at the #Post.

<ex>Police manned the border <fex name="Time">from 3 to 5pm</fex>.</ex>', 'This FE identifies the Means by which the #Agent works at the Post.

<ex>Jason <t>manned</t> the booth <fex name="Means">by handing out brochures and greeting visitors.</fex></ex>', 'Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (<ment>quietly, loudly</ment>), and general descriptions comparing events (<ment>the same way</ment>). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an #Agent that also affect the action (<ment>presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully</ment>). 

<ex>Thanks to our volunteers for <fex name="Manner">tirelessly</fex> <t>manning</t> the entrance all night.</ex>']
